  netting_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collateral.netting_set. Business justification: Clearing records for netted collateral settlements must reference the netting set that determines the net settlement obligation. This link supports multilateral netting position calculation, CCP margi']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.offering. Business justification: Securities offering settlement (IPO, bond issuance) generates clearing records that must be linked to the offering for settlement confirmation, proceeds reconciliation, and regulatory filing (SEC, FCA']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: A clearing record is generated from the execution of a payment transaction through the clearing house. The existing instruction_id FK links clearing_record to the originating instruction, but the dire']
  product_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.product_type. Business justification: Clearing records are product-type-specific (securities clearing, payment clearing, FX clearing) with distinct regulatory reporting requirements and collateral rules. clearing_record has clearing_messa']
  regulatory_reporting_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Clearing records for cross-border transactions require jurisdiction-specific regulatory reporting to local central banks and BIS. clearing_record has regulatory_reporting_flag but no jurisdiction FK. ']
  repo_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treasury.repo_transaction. Business justification: Repo transactions generate clearing records for their cash settlement legs. Linking clearing_record to repo_transaction enables operations to track the clearing status of each repo cash leg, a standar']
  risk_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_limit. Business justification: Clearing records represent intraday settlement obligations subject to settlement risk limits and intraday liquidity limits. Risk teams monitor clearing exposure against limits for large-value payment ']
  security_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.instrument. Business justification: DVP clearing records must link to the instrument for securities settlement netting, CCP margin calculation, and settlement finality confirmation. Critical for central counterparty clearing operations.']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.settlement_instruction. Business justification: Clearing records for trade-driven payments must reference the governing trade settlement instruction for daily settlement reconciliation, break identification, and regulatory settlement reporting. Ban']
  syndication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.investment_syndication. Business justification: Syndicated loan funding settlements pass through clearing. Linking clearing_record to investment_syndication allows the syndication desk to confirm all lender allocations have cleared and to reconcile']
  tranche_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.tranche. Business justification: Each tranche drawdown in syndicated lending generates a clearing record. Linking clearing_record to tranche enables loan operations to confirm tranche-level clearing completion and reconcile clearing ']
  underwriting_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.underwriting. Business justification: Underwriting commitments must clear through the clearing system. Linking clearing_record to underwriting enables the syndicate desk to confirm underwriting commitments are fully settled and to track r']
  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Status of AML (Anti-Money Laundering) screening performed on this clearing record. Indicates whether the transaction has been cleared, flagged for review, is pending screening, or has been escalated for investigation.']
  clearing_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the clearing house confirmed acceptance or rejection of the clearing submission. Marks the completion of the clearing validation phase.']
  clearing_cycle_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific clearing cycle or batch window in which this clearing record was processed. Clearing houses typically operate multiple cycles per day.']
  clearing_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the clearing house for processing this clearing record. May be a flat fee per transaction or a percentage of the cleared amount.']
  clearing_message_type varchar [note: 'Type of clearing message or instruction format used. Examples include SWIFT MT message types (MT103, MT202), ISO 20022 message types (pacs.008, pacs.009), or proprietary clearing formats.']
  clearing_participant_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the financial institution participating in the clearing process. May be a BIC (Bank Identifier Code), ABA routing number, or clearing house member identifier.']
  clearing_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned by the clearing house to identify this clearing submission. Business identifier for external reconciliation and inquiry.']
  clearing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the clearing record. Indicates whether the clearing submission has been accepted, rejected, is pending processing, or has progressed to settlement.']
  clearing_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment instruction or batch was submitted to the clearing house for processing. Represents the business event time of clearing initiation.']
  collateral_requirement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of collateral required to be posted to the clearing house to cover settlement risk exposure. Common in securities clearing and derivatives clearing.']
  counterparty_participant_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the counterparty financial institution on the other side of the clearing transaction. Used in bilateral netting and settlement reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this clearing record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance tracking.']
  cross_border_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this clearing record involves cross-border payment flows between different countries or currency zones. Relevant for FATCA (Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act) and CRS (Common Reporting Standard) compliance.']
  foreign_exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied for cross-currency clearing and settlement, particularly relevant for CLS (Continuous Linked Settlement) FX transactions. Represents the rate at which one currency is converted to another.']
  funding_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number of the nostro, vostro, or settlement account used to fund the net settlement obligation or receive settlement proceeds. Typically a central bank account or correspondent banking account.']
  gross_clearing_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount of all payment instructions included in this clearing record before any netting or offsetting. Represents the sum of all individual payment values submitted.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this clearing record was last updated or modified. Audit field for change tracking and data governance.']
  liquidity_reserve_requirement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of liquidity reserves that must be maintained to cover this clearing obligation. Relevant for regulatory liquidity management and LCR (Liquidity Coverage Ratio) calculations.']
  multilateral_netting_position varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is a net payer (owes funds), net receiver (receives funds), or neutral (no net obligation) after multilateral netting across all participants in the clearing cycle.']
  net_settlement_obligation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net settlement obligation amount after multilateral or bilateral netting has been applied by the clearing house. This is the actual amount that must be funded or will be received in the settlement phase.']
  netting_group_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the netting group or pool to which this clearing record belongs. Used in multilateral netting arrangements where participants are grouped for offset calculation.']
  nostro_vostro_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement account is a nostro account (our account held at another bank), vostro account (their account held at our bank), or an internal account. Critical for correspondent banking reconciliation.']
  payment_instruction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual payment instructions included in this clearing record. Relevant for batch clearing submissions where multiple payments are cleared together.']
  priority_indicator varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this clearing record. High-priority payments may be processed in earlier clearing cycles or given preferential treatment in settlement queues.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this clearing record must be included in regulatory reporting submissions such as CCAR (Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review), DFAST (Dodd-Frank Act Stress Testing), or other supervisory reports.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for clearing rejection if the clearing status is rejected. Examples include insufficient funds, invalid account, format errors, or compliance holds.']
  rejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the reason for clearing rejection, providing additional context beyond the standardized rejection reason code.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Status of sanctions screening against OFAC (Office of Foreign Assets Control) and other sanctions lists. Indicates whether the transaction has been cleared, matched a sanctions list (hit), is pending review, or has been manually overridden.']
  settlement_confirmation_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference number provided by the settlement system confirming that final funds movement has been completed. Used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which final settlement of funds is scheduled to occur. Typically follows T+0, T+1, or T+2 convention depending on the payment system and instrument type.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used for final settlement of the net obligation. RTGS (Real-Time Gross Settlement) settles each transaction individually in real-time. DNS (Deferred Net Settlement) settles net positions at scheduled intervals. Hybrid combines both approaches.']
  settlement_risk_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated settlement risk exposure amount representing the potential loss if the counterparty fails to settle. Used for credit risk management and regulatory capital calculations.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Final status of the settlement process. Indicates whether funds have been successfully transferred, settlement failed, or the transaction was reversed or suspended.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when final settlement of funds was completed and confirmed by the clearing house or settlement system. Marks the irrevocable transfer of funds.']
  settlement_window_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific settlement window or time slot in which final settlement is scheduled. Clearing houses may operate multiple settlement windows throughout the day.']
  source_system_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system that originated this clearing record. Examples include ACI Worldwide, Volante, or internal payment processing platforms. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Represents the clearing-house submission, netting, and resulting settlement obligation for a payment or batch of payments. Captures clearing house identifier (ACH operator, CHIPS, Fedwire, CHAPS, TARGET2, CLS), clearing cycle, net settlement obligation amount, multilateral netting position, clearing confirmation reference, clearing timestamp, settlement date, funding account, settlement method, settlement confirmation reference, and final settlement status. Bridges payment instructions through clearing to final funds movement. SSOT for the complete clearing-to-settlement lifecycle including CLS for FX settlement.'
}

Table payment.routing {
  routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment routing rule. Primary key for the payment routing configuration.']
  correspondent_bank_id varchar [note: 'FK to payment.correspondent_bank']
  destination_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Routing rules vary by destination country for cost optimization (local rails vs SWIFT), speed (RTGS availability), and regulatory compliance (sanctions screening, reporting thresholds). Country master']
  destination_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Routing optimized per currency based on nostro liquidity, correspondent bank relationships, and clearing costs. Currency master provides convertibility status, restrictions, and settlement lag for rou']
  fallback_routing_rule_routing_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an alternative routing rule to use if this primary routing path fails or is unavailable.']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.holiday_calendar. Business justification: Routing cutoff times adjusted for holidays in destination market. Holiday calendar provides partial day hours for same-day payment eligibility and next business day calculation for value dating and SL']
  intermediary_bic_directory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.bic_directory. Business justification: Routing rules reference specific intermediary bank BICs that must be validated against the BIC directory for routing accuracy. routing has intermediary_bank_bic as plain text denormalized field. Banki']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Payment routing rules are jurisdiction-specific — SEPA rules apply in EU, Fedwire rules in US, CHAPS in UK. routing has rule_code and payment_rail_code as plain text but no jurisdiction FK. Banking pa']
  nostro_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to treasury.nostro_account']
  payment_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_channel. Business justification: payment_routing has payment_rail_code which should reference payment_channel. Routing rules are defined per payment channel/rail. This is a standard N:1 relationship where multiple routing rules can b']
  product_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.product_type. Business justification: Routing rules are defined per payment product type — wire transfers, ACH, SEPA, and SWIFT each have distinct routing logic, SLA targets, and regulatory requirements. routing has payment_rail_code as p']
  aml_monitoring_level varchar [note: 'Level of AML transaction monitoring applied to payments using this routing path, based on risk assessment.']
  approval_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Payment amount threshold above which manual approval or additional authorization is required before routing.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing rule was approved for production use, supporting maker-checker controls.']
  cost_basis_points decimal(8,2) [note: 'Cost of using this routing path expressed in basis points (BPS) of the payment amount. Used for cost-optimized routing decisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing rule record was first created in the system.']
  cutoff_time_local varchar [note: 'Daily cut-off time in local time zone (HH:MM:SS format) by which payment instructions must be received to be included in the current settlement cycle.']
  cutoff_timezone varchar [note: 'Time zone identifier for the cut-off time (e.g., EST, GMT, CET) to ensure accurate cross-border timing.']
  daily_aggregate_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative amount allowed per day through this routing path. Used for liquidity management and risk control.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this routing rule becomes effective and eligible for use in payment routing decisions.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this routing rule remains effective. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no expiration.']
  engine_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the payment routing engine configuration that created or last modified this rule.']
  intermediary_bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the intermediary bank, if applicable, for operational transparency and audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing rule record was last updated or modified.']
  liquidity_reserve_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing path requires pre-funded liquidity reserves to be maintained for settlement assurance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, special instructions, or operational guidance for this routing rule.']
  payment_rail_code varchar [note: 'The payment rail or network through which the payment is routed. Determines the clearing and settlement infrastructure used. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ACH|WIRE|RTGS|SWIFT|CARD|SEPA|FASTER_PAYMENTS|TARGET2 — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for this routing rule when multiple rules match a payment instruction. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rail_settlement_model varchar [note: 'Settlement model used by the payment rail: gross settlement (transaction-by-transaction) or net settlement (batch netting).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the routing rule used for operational reference and configuration management.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the purpose and scope of the routing rule.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing rule. Only ACTIVE rules are used for live payment routing decisions.']
  sanctions_screening_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payments routed via this path must undergo OFAC and sanctions screening before execution.']
  settlement_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Settlement cycle indicating when funds are finalized. T+0 is same-day, T+1 is next business day, real-time is immediate.']
  single_transaction_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed for a single payment transaction through this routing path. Enforces per-transaction risk controls.']
  sla_target_completion_minutes varchar [note: 'Target completion time in minutes as defined in the service level agreement for this routing path.']
  speed_minutes varchar [note: 'Expected end-to-end processing time in minutes for payments routed via this path, used for speed-optimized routing.']
  swift_message_type varchar [note: 'SWIFT message type used for this routing path (e.g., MT103 for customer credit transfer, MT202 for financial institution transfer, or ISO 20022 MX messages). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MT103|MT202|MT202COV|MT103_STP|MT199|MT299|MX_PACS008|MX_PACS009 — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  velocity_limit_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of payments allowed through this routing path within the velocity window. Used for risk management and fraud prevention.']
  velocity_window_minutes varchar [note: 'Time window in minutes over which the velocity limit count is enforced. Supports sliding-window velocity checks.']
  vostro_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number of the correspondent banks vostro account held at this bank, if applicable for bilateral settlement.']

  Note: 'Defines the routing rules and correspondent banking paths used to direct payments to their destination across all supported payment rails. Captures routing rule identifier, payment rail code (ACH/wire/RTGS/SWIFT/card), rail settlement model (gross/net), settlement cycle, cut-off times, destination country/currency, correspondent bank BIC, intermediary bank BIC, routing priority, velocity limits, approval thresholds, effective date range, and rule status. Sourced from SWIFT Reference Data (BIC directory, SSI registry) and Volante payment hub routing engine. Enables dynamic routing decisions based on cost, speed, liquidity position, and regulatory requirements.'
}

Table payment.beneficiary {
  beneficiary_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment beneficiary record. Primary key.']
  bank_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Beneficiary bank country drives payment routing (SWIFT vs local rails), sanctions screening (OFAC, EU), correspondent bank selection, and regulatory reporting (FFIEC 009 cross-border). Bank_country_co']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Beneficiary account currency determines payment routing, FX requirements, and correspondent bank selection. Currency master provides convertibility status, restrictions, and settlement lag for payment']
  registered_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: When beneficiary is existing customer, party link enables streamlined KYC (reuse existing due diligence), enhanced sanctions screening (leverage customer risk rating), and simplified beneficiary regis']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The account number at the beneficiary\'s financial institution where funds will be credited. Format varies by country and institution.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary address line of the beneficiary (street address, building number). Required for compliance and verification purposes.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line of the beneficiary (suite, apartment, additional location details).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this beneficiary was approved for payment processing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this beneficiary for payment processing. Required for audit trail and compliance.']
  bank_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary address line of the beneficiary\'s bank or branch (street address, building number).']
  bank_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line of the beneficiary\'s bank or branch (suite, floor, additional location details).']
  bank_branch_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the specific branch where the beneficiary account is held, if applicable.']
  bank_city varchar [note: 'City where the beneficiary\'s bank or branch is located.']
  bank_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the beneficiary\'s bank or branch location.']
  bank_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the beneficiary\'s bank or branch is located.']
  beneficiary_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the beneficiary record. Active beneficiaries can receive payments; suspended or blocked beneficiaries cannot.']
  beneficiary_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the beneficiary entity: individual person, corporate entity, financial institution, government body, non-profit organization, or trust.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the beneficiary is located or registered.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the beneficiary\'s residence or registration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this beneficiary record was first created in the system.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for beneficiary communication and payment notifications.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number for beneficiaries in IBAN-participating countries. Standardized format for cross-border payments.']
  intermediary_bank_bic varchar [note: 'BIC/SWIFT code of the intermediary (correspondent) bank used to route payments to the beneficiary bank, if required.']
  intermediary_bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the intermediary (correspondent) bank, if required for payment routing.']
  intermediary_bank_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an intermediary (correspondent) bank is required to route payments to this beneficiary. True for certain cross-border payments.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment successfully sent to this beneficiary.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the beneficiary as registered with their financial institution or government authority. For individuals, includes full given and family names; for entities, the registered business name.']
  pep_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the beneficiary is a Politically Exposed Person (PEP), Relative or Close Associate (RCA) of a PEP, or non-PEP. Used for enhanced due diligence requirements.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the beneficiary. Used for verification and communication purposes.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the beneficiary\'s address.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the beneficiary was first registered in the payment system.']
  routing_code varchar [note: 'Domestic routing code for the beneficiary\'s bank (e.g., ABA routing number, UK sort code, Australian BSB). Format varies by country.']
  routing_code_type varchar [note: 'Type of domestic routing code used for the beneficiary bank: ABA (US), Sort Code (UK), BSB (Australia), IFSC (India), CLABE (Mexico), Transit Number (Canada), or other branch code. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aba|sort_code|bsb|ifsc|clabe|transit|branch_code — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the beneficiary is located or registered.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number of the beneficiary (e.g., SSN, EIN, VAT number). Required for certain payment types and regulatory reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this beneficiary record was last modified.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the beneficiary account details were last successfully verified.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of beneficiary account verification. Indicates whether the account details have been validated through micro-deposits, API verification, or manual review.']

  Note: 'Master registry of payment beneficiaries (individuals, corporations, and financial institutions) to whom the bank\'s customers direct payments. Captures beneficiary name, account number, IBAN, BIC/SWIFT code, bank name, bank address, country, currency, beneficiary type, verification status, and sanctions screening result. SSOT for beneficiary identity in the payment domain, distinct from the customer domain which owns payer identity.'
}

Table payment.payment_mandate {
  payment_mandate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment mandate record. Primary key.']
  authorizing_account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_holder. Business justification: Direct debit mandate authorization requires knowing the specific account holder role and signing authority level. Mandate dispute resolution, regulatory audit trails (PSD2/NACHA), and mandate validity']
  creditor_beneficiary_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.beneficiary. Business justification: payment_mandate has creditor_name and creditor_account_id. The creditor in a direct debit mandate is a beneficiary in the payment domain. This should be normalized to a FK to beneficiary.beneficiary_i']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: SEPA Direct Debit and BACS mandates specify collection currency. Currency master provides minor unit for maximum amount validation, convertibility status for cross-border mandates, and regulatory repo']
  debtor_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Direct debit mandates require party-level authorization tracking for regulatory compliance (SEPA, NACHA rules), dispute resolution, and customer consent management. Debtor_name is denormalized party d']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Direct debit mandates are associated with GL accounts for automated posting of collections (e.g., direct debit income, mandate fee income). Required for straight-through processing and financial repor']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.holiday_calendar. Business justification: Mandate collection schedules (next_scheduled_collection_date, collection_frequency) must respect holiday calendars to determine valid collection dates. Banking direct debit operations teams use holida']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Payment mandates are governed by jurisdiction-specific regulations (PSD2 in EU, BACS rules in UK, NACHA in US). payment_mandate has regulatory_reporting_flag but no jurisdiction FK. Banking compliance']
  originating_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.session. Business justification: Mandate creation via digital channels must be tied to the authenticated customer session for PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) audit trail. Regulators require proof of authenticated session at']
  payment_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_channel. Business justification: A standing payment mandate or direct debit authorization is tied to a specific payment rail/channel (e.g., ACH, SEPA Direct Debit, BACS). The payment_channel_id FK links the mandate to the channel def']
  primary_payment_creditor_account_deposit_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the creditor account that will receive collected funds. Links to the account master data.']
  product_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.product_type. Business justification: Payment mandates (direct debits, standing orders) are classified by product type for PSD2 regulatory reporting, fee schedule application, and AML monitoring thresholds. payment_mandate has mandate_typ']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment to the mandate terms. Null if mandate has never been amended.']
  amendment_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this mandate has been amended since original signature. True if mandate terms have been modified with debtor consent.']
  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of Anti-Money Laundering screening performed on the mandate parties. PASSED indicates cleared, FAILED indicates hit requiring investigation, PENDING indicates screening in progress, REVIEW indicates manual review required, EXEMPT indicates mandate type not subject to AML screening.']
  authorization_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the debtor authorized the mandate. PAPER for signed physical form, ELECTRONIC for e-signature, ONLINE for web portal, MOBILE for mobile app, TELEPHONE for phone banking, BRANCH for in-person at branch.']
  authorization_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the authorization event or document. Used for audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date on which the mandate was cancelled or revoked. Populated when mandate status transitions to CANCELLED or REVOKED.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for mandate cancellation or revocation. Captures debtor request, creditor termination, or regulatory reason.']
  collection_frequency varchar [note: 'Expected frequency of payment collections under this mandate. ONCE for one-time mandates, ADHOC for variable timing, or regular intervals for recurring mandates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ONCE|DAILY|WEEKLY|MONTHLY|QUARTERLY|ANNUALLY|ADHOC — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the mandate record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  creditor_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the creditor by the national banking authority or scheme. Used to identify the party authorized to collect payments under this mandate. For SEPA, this is the Creditor Identifier issued by national central banks.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the mandate is currently subject to a dispute or chargeback claim by the debtor. True if active dispute exists.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the mandate expires and can no longer be used for collections. Null indicates open-ended mandate. After this date, mandate status transitions to EXPIRED.']
  last_collection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment collection executed under this mandate. Used to track mandate usage and identify dormant mandates.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the mandate record. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  mandate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment mandate. ACTIVE indicates mandate is valid and can be used for collections. SUSPENDED indicates temporary hold. CANCELLED indicates creditor-initiated termination. EXPIRED indicates mandate passed end date. PENDING indicates awaiting activation. REVOKED indicates debtor-initiated cancellation.']
  mandate_type varchar [note: 'Type of direct debit mandate scheme. SEPA_CORE for consumer direct debits in SEPA zone, SEPA_B2B for business-to-business, ACH_PPD for Prearranged Payment and Deposit Entry, ACH_CCD for Cash Concentration or Disbursement, BACS for UK direct debit, BECS for Australian direct debit, PAD for Canadian Pre-Authorized Debit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SEPA_CORE|SEPA_B2B|ACH_PPD|ACH_CCD|BACS|BECS|PAD — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  maximum_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount that can be collected in a single transaction under this mandate. Null indicates no limit. Used for debtor protection and validation of collection requests.']
  next_scheduled_collection_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned payment collection under this mandate. Used for recurring mandate scheduling and debtor notification.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique mandate reference assigned by the creditor or payment service provider. Used for mandate identification in payment instructions and SEPA direct debit schemes.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this mandate requires special regulatory reporting or monitoring. True for mandates subject to enhanced due diligence or cross-border reporting requirements.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions list screening performed on mandate parties against OFAC, UN, EU, and other watchlists. CLEARED indicates no match, HIT indicates potential match requiring investigation, PENDING indicates screening in progress, REVIEW indicates manual review required.']
  sequence_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the position of the collection in the mandate lifecycle. FIRST for initial collection, RECURRING for subsequent collections, FINAL for last collection, ONEOFF for single-use mandates, REPRESENTMENT for retry after return.']
  signature_date date [note: 'Date on which the debtor signed or authorized the mandate. Used to determine mandate validity and for dispute resolution. Must precede first collection date.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date from which the mandate becomes effective and collections can be initiated. Cannot be earlier than signature date.']

  Note: 'Standing payment mandate or direct debit authorization granted by a customer to a creditor, enabling recurring or one-time pull payments. Captures mandate reference, mandate type (SEPA Core, SEPA B2B, ACH PPD/CCD), creditor identifier, debtor account, maximum amount, frequency, start date, end date, mandate status, and presentment sequence (first/recurring/final/one-off/re-presentment). Supports mandate amendment, cancellation, and dispute workflows. SSOT for pre-authorized payment agreements and direct debit lifecycle management.'
}

Table payment.card_transaction {
  card_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the card transaction record. Primary key.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Card transactions must be attributed to accounting periods for period-end financial close, revenue recognition, and regulatory reporting. Banks need this for accurate period-level card revenue and int']
  alert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_alert. Business justification: Card transactions trigger fraud alerts during authorization and settlement. Direct linkage enables real-time fraud decisioning, chargeback management, and card fraud analytics. Core card fraud detecti']
  atm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.atm. Business justification: Card transactions at ATMs require direct link to ATM entity for transaction reconciliation, dispute resolution (location verification), ATM cash management, performance analytics, and fraud investigat']
  card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.card. Business justification: Every card transaction is performed on a specific physical or virtual card. card_id FK normalizes the relationship to the card master record. card_token and masked_pan are retained as execution-specif']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Card transactions are attributed to cost centers (e.g., card issuing business line) for management accounting, profitability analysis, and internal cost allocation. Banks track card revenue and costs ']
  ctr_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ctr_filing. Business justification: Cash-equivalent card transactions (ATM withdrawals, cash advances) may require CTR filing when exceeding $10,000 threshold. Links transaction to CTR for BSA compliance and aggregation across multiple ']
  customer_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.session. Business justification: Card transactions initiated through digital channels (e-commerce, mobile wallet) need session context for fraud detection (device fingerprint, behavioral biometrics), 3D Secure authentication validati']
  deposit_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the card account used for this transaction.']
  exchange_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.exchange_rate. Business justification: Cross-border card transactions use a specific exchange rate for billing amount calculation and dispute resolution. card_transaction has fx_rate as a plain numeric field but no FK to the authoritative ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Card transactions post to specific GL accounts (interchange income, fee income, suspense). The GL account link is required for automated posting, financial reporting, and reconciliation of card settle']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Card transactions must be booked to the issuing legal entity for regulatory reporting, entity-level P&L, and capital adequacy calculations. Required for FINREP reporting and entity-level card portfoli']
  merchant_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Merchant country determines interchange rates, fraud velocity rules, 3DS requirements (PSD2 SCA), and cross-border transaction fees. Country master provides sanctions status, AML risk rating, and regu']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the merchant by the acquirer or card network.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cardholder (customer) who initiated the transaction.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: A card transaction generates a payment_transaction record for settlement processing through the clearing and settlement cycle. Linking card_transaction to payment_transaction enables end-to-end tracea']
  product_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.product_type. Business justification: Card transactions are classified by product type (credit, debit, prepaid, corporate) for interchange fee calculation, regulatory reporting, and fraud monitoring rule application. card_transaction has ']
  transaction_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Card transaction currency (merchant currency) drives DCC eligibility, interchange calculation, and fraud scoring. Currency master provides minor unit for amount precision and cross-border indicator fo']
  acquirer_bic varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC code of the acquiring bank that processed the merchant transaction.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Approval code returned by the card issuer when the transaction is authorized.']
  authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transaction authorization was received from the card issuer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  avs_result_code varchar [note: 'Result code from address verification system comparing billing address with issuer records.']
  billing_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount billed to the cardholder in the card account currency after foreign exchange conversion if applicable.']
  billing_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the billing amount (card account currency).']
  card_present_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the physical card was present at the time of transaction (True) or not present for card-not-present transactions (False).']
  card_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized representation of the card number used for secure processing without exposing actual PAN.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Type of card used for the transaction: debit, credit, prepaid, or charge card.']
  cardholder_present_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder was physically present at the point of sale (True) or not (False).']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Channel through which the transaction was initiated: point of sale, ATM, e-commerce, mobile app, phone, or mail order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this transaction record was first created in the payment processing system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  cvv_result_code varchar [note: 'Result of CVV verification: M=Match, N=No Match, P=Not Processed, S=Should be present, U=Unsupported.']
  decline_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for transaction decline if authorization was not approved (e.g., insufficient funds, invalid card, suspected fraud).']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was flagged as potentially fraudulent by the fraud detection system (True) or not (False).']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned by fraud detection system indicating likelihood of fraudulent activity, typically 0-100 scale.']
  fx_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency to billing currency for cross-border transactions.']
  interchange_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Interchange fee charged by the card issuer to the acquirer for processing the transaction.']
  issuer_bic varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC code of the card issuing bank.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this transaction record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  masked_pan varchar [note: 'Masked card number showing first 6 and last 4 digits with middle digits obscured for security. Format: 123456******7890.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit code classifying the merchants business type (e.g., 5411 for grocery stores, 5812 for restaurants).']
  merchant_city varchar [note: 'City where the merchant is located or where the transaction occurred.']
  network_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned by the card network for tracking and reconciliation across the payment lifecycle.']
  pos_entry_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which the card data was entered at the point of sale: chip, swipe, contactless, manual entry, e-commerce, or mobile wallet.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the transaction was posted to the cardholders account. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  retrieval_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number used to retrieve transaction details from the card network or processor.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the transaction is scheduled to settle between acquirer and issuer, typically T+1. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  three_d_secure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3D Secure authentication was used for this transaction (True) or not (False).']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original transaction amount in the transaction currency before any fees or adjustments.']
  transaction_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique external reference number assigned by the card network or acquiring system for this transaction.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the card transaction in the processing workflow.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the card transaction was initiated at the point of sale or online channel. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of card transaction: purchase, refund, chargeback, reversal, cash advance, or balance inquiry.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for card-based payments including debit, credit, and prepaid card transactions processed through card networks (Visa, Mastercard, Amex). Captures card number (masked PAN), merchant ID, merchant category code (MCC), transaction amount, authorization code, acquirer BIC, network reference, transaction type (purchase/refund/chargeback), and settlement status. Sourced from card processing module of ACI Worldwide.'
}

Table payment.sanction_screening {
  sanction_screening_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment sanction screening record.']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.deal. Business justification: Investment banking deals require sanctions screening of all deal parties. Linking sanction_screening to deal enables compliance teams to confirm deal-level sanctions clearance, satisfy OFAC/EU sanctio']
  deal_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.deal_participant. Business justification: Each deal participant must be individually sanctions-screened. Linking sanction_screening to deal_participant provides a direct audit trail of participant-level screening results, required for KYC/AML']
  instruction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment instruction that was screened against sanctions watchlists.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Sanctions screening is jurisdiction-specific — OFAC (US), OFSI (UK), and EU sanctions regimes have distinct watchlists and regulatory filing requirements. sanction_screening has ofac_blocking_report_r']
  monitoring_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.monitoring_rule. Business justification: Sanctions screening events are triggered by specific monitoring rules in banking compliance systems. Direct FK from sanction_screening to monitoring_rule supports audit trails showing which rule versi']
  sanctions_screening_event_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the sanctioned entity on the watchlist (e.g., OFAC SDN number, UN reference number) that was matched.']
  screened_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: AML compliance requires linking screening results to specific customer party for audit trail, regulatory examination, SAR filing decisions, and customer risk rating updates. Critical for OFAC/sanction']
  analyst_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision made by the AML analyst after reviewing the sanctions alert: approve payment, reject payment, mark as false positive, or escalate to Money Laundering Reporting Officer (MLRO).']
  analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and rationale documented by the AML analyst explaining their review findings and decision for audit and regulatory purposes.']
  analyst_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the screening result requires manual review by an Anti-Money Laundering (AML) analyst due to potential match or high-risk indicators.']
  analyst_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the AML analyst completed their review of the sanctions screening alert.']
  auto_decision_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the screening decision was made automatically by the system (true) or required manual analyst review (false).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sanctions screening record was first created in the system.']
  false_positive_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a sanctions match was determined to be a false positive: common name, different jurisdiction, different date of birth, different address, whitelist match, or other.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sanctions screening record was last updated, typically after analyst review or decision change.']
  match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence score (0-100) indicating the strength of the match between the payment party and the watchlist entry, with higher scores indicating stronger matches.']
  match_status varchar [note: 'Overall result of the sanctions screening indicating whether a match was found: no match, potential match requiring review, confirmed match, or false positive after analyst review.']
  match_type varchar [note: 'Type of matching algorithm that identified the potential sanctions hit: exact name match, fuzzy match, partial match, phonetic match, or alias match.']
  matched_entity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity on the sanctions watchlist that matched or partially matched the payment party information.']
  matched_field varchar [note: 'Specific field in the payment instruction that triggered the sanctions match: name, address, identifier, account number, Bank Identifier Code (BIC), or International Bank Account Number (IBAN).']
  matched_party_role varchar [note: 'Role of the payment party that triggered the sanctions match: originator, beneficiary, intermediary bank, or correspondent bank.']
  ofac_blocking_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the blocking report submitted to Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) for confirmed sanctions matches requiring asset blocking.']
  ofac_blocking_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment must be blocked and reported to Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) due to confirmed match with a sanctioned entity under United States sanctions programs.']
  regulatory_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sanctions screening result requires regulatory reporting such as Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) filing to Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) or equivalent authority.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the sanctions screening was retried due to system errors or timeouts before a final result was obtained.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to this sanctions screening result based on match score, watchlist type, party role, and other risk factors.']
  sar_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) was filed with the regulatory authority in response to this sanctions screening result.']
  sar_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) filed with Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) or other regulatory authority if this screening triggered a filing obligation.']
  screening_decision varchar [note: 'Automated or manual decision resulting from the sanctions screening: pass (allow payment), hold (suspend for review), block (reject payment), or escalate (send to compliance officer).']
  screening_duration_ms varchar [note: 'Time taken to complete the sanctions screening process measured in milliseconds, used for system performance monitoring and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.']
  screening_error_code varchar [note: 'Error code returned by the sanctions screening system if the screening process failed or encountered technical issues.']
  screening_error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed error message describing any technical issues encountered during the sanctions screening process.']
  screening_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the sanctions screening system for audit trail and case management.']
  screening_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and sanctions screening system that performed the check (e.g., NICE Actimize, Oracle Financial Crime).']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sanctions screening was performed against the payment instruction.']
  watchlist_type varchar [note: 'Type of sanctions or watchlist screened against: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Specially Designated Nationals (SDN), European Union (EU) Consolidated List, United Nations (UN) Sanctions, Politically Exposed Persons (PEP), Her Majesty\'s Treasury (HMT) UK, or Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT) Australia.']
  watchlist_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier or effective date of the sanctions watchlist used during screening to ensure audit trail and regulatory compliance.']

  Note: 'Records the OFAC/sanctions screening result for each payment instruction against watchlists (OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, UN Sanctions, PEP lists). Captures screening timestamp, list version, match score, match type (exact/fuzzy/partial), matched entity name, screening decision (pass/hold/block), analyst review outcome, and regulatory reporting obligation. Supports BSA/AML compliance for payment processing.'
}

  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Status of anti-money laundering screening performed on this FX transaction and its counterparties.']

  base_currency_settlement_account varchar [note: 'Account number or identifier for the nostro/vostro account used to settle the base currency leg of the FX transaction.']
  booking_entity varchar [note: 'Legal entity or branch of the institution that booked this FX transaction on its balance sheet.']
  buy_sell_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the institution is buying or selling the base currency in this FX transaction.']

  currency_pair varchar [note: 'Standard representation of the currency pair being traded (e.g., USD/EUR, GBP/JPY).']
  deal_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The agreed exchange rate at which the base currency is converted to the quote currency for this transaction.']
  deal_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this FX deal by the trading system or counterparty.']
  deal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the FX deal was executed or agreed upon between parties.']

  purpose_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory or business purpose code indicating the reason for the FX transaction (e.g., trade finance, investment, hedging).']
  quote_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The equivalent amount in the quote currency calculated using the deal rate.']
  quote_currency_settlement_account varchar [note: 'Account number or identifier for the nostro/vostro account used to settle the quote currency leg of the FX transaction.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this FX transaction is subject to regulatory reporting requirements (e.g., EMIR, Dodd-Frank, MiFID II).']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Status of sanctions screening performed against OFAC, UN, EU, and other watchlists for this FX transaction.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net cash settlement amount paid or received for non-deliverable forwards, calculated as the difference between deal rate and fixing rate.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Actual date when the FX transaction settled and currency exchange was completed.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to settle the FX transaction: gross settlement, multilateral netting, or Continuous Linked Settlement (CLS).']
  spot_rate_reference decimal(18,8) [note: 'The prevailing spot exchange rate at the time of deal execution, used as reference for forward pricing.']
  swift_message_type varchar [note: 'SWIFT message type used to communicate this FX transaction (e.g., MT300 for FX confirmation, MT202 for payment).']
  swift_uetr varchar [note: 'Unique end-to-end transaction reference assigned by SWIFT for tracking this FX transaction across the payment chain.']
  trade_date date [note: 'The date on which the FX transaction was executed in the market.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the FX transaction in the payment processing workflow.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of FX transaction: spot (immediate settlement), forward (future settlement), swap (simultaneous buy/sell), or non-deliverable forward (NDF).']
  value_date date [note: 'The date on which the FX transaction is scheduled to settle and funds are exchanged.']

  Note: 'Foreign exchange transaction record for spot, forward, and swap FX trades executed through the payments function. Captures currency pair, deal rate, value date, settlement instructions, and counterparty.'
}

  preferred_intermediary_bic varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC of the preferred intermediary bank to use when routing payments through this correspondent, if applicable.']
  primary_currency varchar [note: 'Primary ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for settlement with this correspondent bank.']
  regulatory_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for regulatory approval documentation related to this correspondent relationship.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory approval or notification is required for establishing or maintaining this correspondent relationship.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when the correspondent banking relationship was terminated or is scheduled to terminate. Null for active relationships.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when the correspondent banking relationship became effective and operational for payment routing.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the correspondent banking relationship indicating operational availability and compliance standing.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Type of correspondent banking relationship: nostro (our account with them), vostro (their account with us), bilateral (reciprocal), or unilateral (one-way).']
  service_level_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the Service Level Agreement (SLA) governing operational performance and service standards with this correspondent.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Standard settlement cycle for transactions: T0 (same day), T1 (trade date plus one day), T2 (trade date plus two days).']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used for settling transactions with the correspondent: gross (real-time transaction-by-transaction), net (end-of-day netting), or deferred_net (periodic netting).']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 4217 three-letter currency codes that can be processed through this correspondent relationship (e.g., USD,EUR,GBP,JPY).']
  swift_message_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SWIFT message types (MT codes) supported for this correspondent relationship (e.g., MT103, MT202, MT910).']
  vostro_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number of the correspondent bank\'s vostro account held at our institution for reciprocal settlement.']

  Note: 'Master registry of correspondent banking relationships used for cross-border payment routing. Captures correspondent BIC, relationship type, supported currencies, credit limits, and due diligence status.'
}

Table payment.payment_channel {
  payment_channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment channel. Primary key for the payment channel master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Payment channels (SWIFT, ACH, SEPA) are attributed to cost centers for channel cost allocation, management accounting, and channel profitability reporting. Banks track the cost of operating each payme']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Payment channels have associated fee income and cost GL accounts for channel-level P&L reporting and automated fee posting. Banks track channel economics at the GL level for management reporting and f']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.holiday_calendar. Business justification: Payment channel availability tied to market holidays (e.g., CHAPS closed on UK bank holidays, Fedwire closed on US federal holidays). Holiday calendar provides operating hours and partial day schedule']
  parent_payment_channel_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_channel (parent_payment_channel_id)']
  primary_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Payment channels optimized for specific currency (e.g., SEPA for EUR, CHAPS for GBP). Currency master provides settlement cycle, cutoff times, and liquidity requirements for channel capacity planning ']
  aml_screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payments through this channel must undergo mandatory Anti-Money Laundering screening before processing.']
  batch_processing_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment channel supports batch file submission for multiple payment instructions in a single transmission.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the payment rail or channel (e.g., ACH, WIRE, SWIFT, SEPA, RTP, FEDNOW, CHIPS, VISA, MASTERCARD).']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the payment channel or rail (e.g., Automated Clearing House, Fedwire Funds Service, Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication).']
  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the payment channel indicating availability for payment processing.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment channel based on processing model: batch (ACH), real-time (RTP, FedNow), near real-time (SWIFT gpi), or deferred settlement.']
  charge_bearer_default varchar [note: 'Default charge bearer code for the channel indicating who pays transaction fees: OUR (originator pays all), BEN (beneficiary pays all), SHA (shared between parties).']
  clearing_house_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the clearing house or settlement system associated with this payment channel (e.g., FedACH, CHIPS, CLS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this payment channel record was first created in the system, expressed in ISO 8601 format with timezone.']
  cross_border_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment channel supports cross-border international payments (True) or is limited to domestic transactions (False).']
  cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Daily deadline for submitting payments to be included in the current settlement cycle, expressed in HH:MM format in the operating timezone.']
  daily_transaction_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum aggregate payment amount that can be processed through this channel in a single business day, expressed in the primary currency.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this payment channel configuration became active and available for payment processing.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date on which this payment channel configuration will be or was deactivated. Null indicates the channel is currently active with no planned end date.']
  fee_structure_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the fee schedule and pricing structure applicable to payments processed through this channel.']
  gpi_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel supports SWIFT Global Payments Innovation (gpi) for enhanced tracking and faster processing of cross-border payments.']
  intermediary_bank_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment channel supports routing through intermediary correspondent banks for indirect settlement.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this payment channel record was most recently updated, expressed in ISO 8601 format with timezone.']
  maximum_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum payment amount that can be processed through this channel in a single transaction, expressed in the primary currency.']
  minimum_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum payment amount that can be processed through this channel, expressed in the primary currency.']
  network_operator varchar [note: 'The organization or entity that operates and governs the payment network or rail (e.g., Federal Reserve, SWIFT, The Clearing House, NACHA, Visa, Mastercard).']
  nostro_account_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payments through this channel require the use of nostro accounts (our account held at correspondent banks) for settlement.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Daily end time when the payment channel closes for transaction submission, expressed in HH:MM format in the channels operating timezone.']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Daily start time when the payment channel opens for transaction submission, expressed in HH:MM format in the channels operating timezone.']
  operating_timezone varchar [note: 'Timezone in which the payment channel operates and cutoff times are defined (e.g., EST, UTC, CET).']
  priority_levels_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment priority levels supported by this channel (e.g., normal, urgent, high, express) for expedited processing.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payments through this channel trigger mandatory regulatory reporting requirements (e.g., Currency Transaction Reports, Suspicious Activity Reports).']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'The number of business days within which a payment processed through this channel can be returned or reversed by the receiving institution.']
  sanctions_screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payments through this channel must undergo mandatory sanctions screening against OFAC and other watchlists before processing.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Standard settlement timeframe for the payment channel expressed as trade date plus days (T+0, T+1, T+2) or descriptive terms (same-day, next-day, intraday).']
  settlement_model varchar [note: 'Settlement methodology used by the channel: gross settlement (transaction-by-transaction) or net settlement (aggregated positions).']
  sla_target_completion_minutes varchar [note: 'The target time in minutes from payment submission to settlement confirmation as defined in the channels service level agreement.']
  standard_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The standard per-transaction fee charged for processing payments through this channel, expressed in the primary currency.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 4217 three-letter currency codes that the payment channel supports for transaction processing (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  swift_message_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SWIFT message type codes supported by this channel (e.g., MT103, MT202, MT900, MT910) for payment instructions and confirmations.']
  vostro_account_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payments through this channel require the use of vostro accounts (their account held at our bank) for settlement.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the payment channels and rails available for payment processing — ACH, Fedwire, CHIPS, SWIFT, SEPA, card networks (Visa/Mastercard), real-time payment networks (RTP, FedNow). Captures channel code, channel name, operating hours, cut-off times, settlement cycle, and supported currencies.'
}

  exception_count varchar [note: 'The number of exceptions or anomalies identified during the reconciliation process that require investigation or resolution.']
  external_statement_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number or identifier of the external statement, confirmation file, or settlement report being reconciled.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this reconciliation record was last updated or modified.']

  matched_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payment transactions that were successfully matched between internal records and external confirmations.']
  matching_rule_set varchar [note: 'The identifier or name of the rule set or algorithm used to match internal payment records with external confirmations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for analysts to document observations, resolution actions, root causes of breaks, or other relevant information about the reconciliation activity.']
  payment_rail_type varchar [note: 'The payment system or network through which the payments being reconciled were processed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ACH|WIRE|RTGS|SWIFT|SEPA|FEDWIRE|CHIPS|TARGET2|CARD|FX — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The business date on which the reconciliation activity was performed.']
  reconciliation_method varchar [note: 'The method used to perform the reconciliation, indicating the level of automation versus manual intervention.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reconciliation activity in its lifecycle, indicating whether the process is ongoing, completed, or encountered issues.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the reconciliation process was executed.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'The category of reconciliation being performed, indicating the source of external records being matched against internal payment records.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique reference number for the reconciliation activity, used for tracking and audit purposes.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reconciliation activity or its breaks require reporting to regulatory authorities due to materiality or compliance requirements.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'The current status of resolution efforts for identified reconciliation breaks and exceptions.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when all reconciliation breaks were resolved and the reconciliation was closed.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which the payments being reconciled were settled or are expected to settle.']
  sla_actual_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual number of hours taken to complete the reconciliation process from initiation to resolution.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation process exceeded the defined SLA target time, requiring escalation or management attention.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'The target number of hours within which the reconciliation process should be completed according to service level agreements.']
  system_source varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the payment processing system or reconciliation platform that generated this reconciliation record.']
  tolerance_threshold_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum acceptable variance in monetary amounts for a match to be considered successful during reconciliation.']
  total_payment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of payment transactions included in the reconciliation scope.']
  total_reconciled_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'The aggregate monetary value of all payments included in the reconciliation scope.']
  unmatched_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'The aggregate monetary value of payments that could not be matched, representing the financial exposure of reconciliation breaks.']
  unmatched_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payment transactions that could not be matched between internal records and external confirmations, representing reconciliation breaks.']
  value_date date [note: 'The effective date for interest calculation and availability of funds for the payments being reconciled.']

  Note: 'Operational record of payment reconciliation activities matching internal payment records against external clearing house confirmations, correspondent bank statements, and card network settlement files. Captures reconciliation date, matched/unmatched counts, break items, and resolution status.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for card. Referenced by card_id.'
}

  supports_cross_border_payments boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the merchant is enabled to accept payments from international customers.']
  supports_recurring_payments boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the merchant is enabled to process recurring or subscription-based payments.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identifier for the merchant entity (e.g., EIN, VAT number).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the merchant relationship was terminated or the account was closed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the merchant record was last modified.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL where the merchant conducts e-commerce or displays business information.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for merchant. Referenced by merchant_id.'
}

Table payment.reconciliation_item {
  reconciliation_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the reconciliation_item association']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific payment transaction being matched in this reconciliation run.']
  reconciliation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the reconciliation run in which this transaction was processed.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this reconciliation item raised an exception during the matching process, requiring manual investigation or escalation by the reconciliation team.']
  match_status varchar [note: 'The outcome of the matching process for this specific transaction within this reconciliation run. Indicates whether the transaction was fully matched, partially matched, unmatched (break), or subsequently resolved.']
  matched_amount decimal [note: 'The monetary value confirmed as matched between the internal payment transaction record and the external statement entry for this specific reconciliation item. May differ from the transaction amount in partial match scenarios.']
  reconciliation_sequence varchar [note: 'Indicates which cycle of the reconciliation process this item belongs to — initial run, amended run (after additional statement data arrives), or final run (after all breaks are resolved). Enables tracking of a transaction across multiple reconciliation cycles.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the matching Event between a reconciliation run and a specific payment transaction. It captures the operational outcome of matching one payment transaction within one reconciliation cycle, including match status, matched amount, sequence, and exception indicators. Each record is created by the reconciliation engine when it processes a transaction against an external statement, and is updated as breaks are investigated and resolved. This is the SSOT for per-transaction reconciliation outcomes.. Existence Justification: In payment operations, reconciliation is a batch process that matches multiple payment transactions against external clearing/settlement statements. A single reconciliation run covers many transactions, and critically, the same transaction can appear across multiple reconciliation cycles (initial, amended, final) — meaning the relationship is genuinely bidirectional and many-to-many. The business actively manages \'reconciliation items\' — named operational records that capture the matching result (match_status, exception_flag, matched_amount, reconciliation_sequence) for each specific transaction within each specific reconciliation run.'
}

Table security.instrument {
  instrument_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the financial instrument. Primary key for the instrument master record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Every instrument trades in a specific currency. Essential for FX risk calculation, P&L reporting, FRTB market risk capital, and Basel regulatory reporting. Replaces denormalized currency_code with pro']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Under IFRS 9 and ASC 320, each financial instrument maps to a specific GL account (e.g., AFS securities, HTM, trading book). This mapping drives balance sheet classification, P&L reporting, and regula']
  issuance_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Country of issuance determines legal jurisdiction, tax treatment, and regulatory reporting requirements. Critical for sanctions screening, country risk limits, and Basel country exposure reporting. Ro']
  issuer_id varchar [note: 'FK to security.issuer']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Instruments are governed by jurisdiction-specific regulatory frameworks (MiFID II, SEC) determining capital treatment, reporting obligations, and eligible investor rules. A banking regulatory capital ']
  product_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.product_type. Business justification: Instruments must be mapped to reference product types for Basel RWA calculation, IFRS9 portfolio segmentation, FATCA/CRS reportability, and GL account mapping. Banking product control and regulatory r']
  underlying_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying instrument for derivatives and structured products. Links to another instrument record.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the instrument into major asset categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equity|fixed_income|derivative|fx|commodity|structured_product|money_market — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  bloomberg_ticker varchar [note: 'Bloomberg proprietary ticker symbol used to identify the instrument in Bloomberg Terminal systems.']
  callable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the issuer has the right to redeem the instrument before maturity.']
  cfi_code varchar [note: '6-character ISO standard code classifying the instrument by category, group, and attributes.']
  convertible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the instrument can be converted into another security (typically equity).']
  country_of_risk varchar [note: '3-letter ISO country code representing the primary country exposure for risk management purposes.']
