  client_mandate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wealth.client_mandate. Business justification: Coverage bankers in investment banking coordinate with wealth management mandates for the same client, ensuring holistic relationship management. Real business process: cross-LOB client coverage coord']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Coverage bankers are assigned to cost centers; revenue_attribution_pct and origination_credit_pct are allocated against cost centers for banker compensation and profitability reporting. Required for m']
  counterparty_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.counterparty_rating. Business justification: Coverage assignments require current counterparty ratings for relationship management, credit decision authority, and client tier classification. Essential for determining banker approval limits and e']
  coverage_branch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.branch. Business justification: coverage_assignment.coverage_office is a denormalized text field. Normalizing to branch_id enables branch-level revenue attribution, headcount reporting, and regulatory jurisdiction mapping. Banking o']
  coverage_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: CRM and revenue attribution reporting requires knowing which channel (digital portal, phone, in-person) a coverage banker uses to engage a client. Banking CRM systems track coverage channel for client']
  geography_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Coverage territory management, client segmentation by geography, AML risk rating by country, and regulatory reporting of cross-border client relationships require linking coverage assignments to the c']
  industry_code_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the industry sector classification (e.g., TMT, Healthcare, Energy, Financials, Industrials) under which this coverage assignment is organized. Links to the sector reference product.']
  issuer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.issuer. Business justification: Investment banking coverage bankers are assigned to cover specific issuers (corporates, governments). This is the foundational CRM relationship in IB — coverage teams track banker-to-issuer assignment']
  kyc_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.kyc_record. Business justification: Coverage bankers must confirm KYC clearance before client engagement; kyc_status and kyc_expiry_date on coverage_assignment are denormalized from kyc_record. Direct FK enforces KYC-gated coverage acti']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Coverage assignments are governed by compliance obligations including information barrier policies, conflicts of interest rules (FINRA Rule 2010), and MiFID II research unbundling requirements. FK to ']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corporate or institutional client entity being covered. Links to the client/counterparty master record used for KYC and CRM purposes.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.profit_center. Business justification: Coverage assignments drive revenue attribution to profit centers via origination_credit_pct and revenue_attribution_pct. This is the primary mechanism for allocating deal revenue to coverage bankers\' ']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: IB coverage assignments are organized by client segment/tier. client_tier on coverage_assignment is a denormalized copy of customer.segment data. Direct FK enables coverage reporting by segment, suppo']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'The Anti-Money Laundering (AML) risk rating assigned to the client entity for this coverage relationship. Determines the frequency and depth of enhanced due diligence (EDD) reviews required. Sourced from the AML/Financial Crime system.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the coverage assignment was formally approved by the designated approving officer. Part of the governance audit trail for IB coverage decisions.']
  assignment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the coverage assignment, used in pitch books, mandate letters, and CRM systems to uniquely reference this banker-client relationship.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The business event date on which the coverage assignment decision was formally made and recorded, which may differ from the effective_from_date if there is a future-dated assignment.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the coverage assignment. Active indicates the banker is currently covering the client; transferred indicates ownership has moved to another banker; terminated indicates the relationship has ended.']
  banker_title varchar [note: 'The seniority title of the assigned coverage banker at the time of assignment (e.g., Analyst, Associate, Vice President, Director, Managing Director). Relevant for deal origination credit allocation and client relationship governance.']
  coverage_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the coverage banker or coverage manager regarding the client relationship, strategic priorities, or special circumstances of this assignment. Contains sensitive business development information.']
  coverage_role varchar [note: 'The functional role of the assigned banker in the client coverage model. Primary bankers own the overall relationship; secondary bankers provide support; sector and product specialists contribute domain expertise. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary|secondary|sector_specialist|product_specialist|co_coverage|advisory — promote to reference product]']
  coverage_team_name varchar [note: 'Name of the investment banking coverage team or group responsible for this assignment (e.g., \'Global TMT Coverage\', \'Healthcare Investment Banking\'). Supports team-level performance reporting and deal pipeline management.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date on which the coverage assignment became effective and the banker formally assumed responsibility for the client relationship. Used for revenue attribution and accountability tracking.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'The date on which the coverage assignment is scheduled to end or was terminated. Null for open-ended assignments. Used for historical coverage analysis and transition planning.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this coverage assignment is currently active. Derived from assignment_status but maintained as an explicit field to support efficient filtering in analytics queries and reporting dashboards without requiring status string comparisons.']
  is_lead_coverage boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this banker is the designated lead coverage officer for the client relationship. Only one banker per client should have this flag set to True at any given time. Drives primary accountability and escalation routing.']
  is_sector_specialist boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the assigned banker is acting in a sector specialist capacity rather than a generalist relationship management role. Sector specialists provide deep industry expertise and are typically engaged on specific transaction types.']
  last_client_interaction_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the coverage banker had a documented interaction with the client (meeting, call, pitch presentation). Used for CRM activity monitoring and relationship health assessment.']
  lob_name varchar [note: 'The investment banking Line of Business (LOB) associated with this coverage assignment, indicating the primary product focus area (e.g., M&A Advisory, Equity Capital Markets (ECM), Debt Capital Markets (DCM), Leveraged Finance, Syndicated Lending). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: M&A_advisory|ECM|DCM|leveraged_finance|syndicated_lending|restructuring|corporate_finance — promote to reference product]']
  mandate_count varchar [note: 'The number of active IB mandates (M&A, IPO, DCM, etc.) currently associated with this coverage assignment at the time of record. Provides a snapshot of deal pipeline depth for this banker-client relationship.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this coverage assignment, at which point the assignment may be renewed, transferred, or terminated. Supports coverage governance and banker accountability cycles.']
  origination_credit_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of deal origination credit allocated to this banker for revenue attribution purposes. Multiple bankers may share credit across a single client relationship; percentages across all active assignments for a client should sum to 100. Used in RAROC and banker performance measurement.']
  pitch_book_count varchar [note: 'The number of pitch books or marketing presentations delivered to this client under this coverage assignment. Tracks business development activity intensity and supports pipeline conversion analysis.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this coverage assignment record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this coverage assignment record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, SCD processing, and audit compliance.']
  relationship_since_date date [note: 'The date on which the bank first established a formal investment banking relationship with this client entity. Distinct from the coverage assignment effective date; reflects the longevity of the overall client relationship.']
  revenue_attribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of total client-generated IB revenue attributed to this specific coverage assignment. Distinct from origination_credit_pct as it may reflect ongoing relationship management credit rather than deal origination credit alone.']
  sector_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the industry sector under which this coverage assignment is classified (e.g., TMT, Healthcare, Energy, Financials, Industrials). Aligns with GICS sector taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TMT|healthcare|energy|financials|industrials|consumer|real_estate|utilities|materials — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this coverage assignment record was sourced (e.g., CRM platform, IB deal management system). Supports data lineage and ETL reconciliation in the lakehouse.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier of this coverage assignment in the originating operational system of record. Enables traceability from the Silver Layer back to the source system for audit and reconciliation.']
  sub_sector_name varchar [note: 'More granular sub-sector classification within the primary sector (e.g., within TMT: Software, Semiconductors, Media; within Healthcare: Pharma, MedTech, Biotech). Supports specialist banker routing and deal origination attribution.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the coverage assignment was terminated or transferred. Populated only when assignment_status is terminated or transferred. Distinct from effective_until_date which may be a planned end date.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the coverage assignment was terminated or transferred. Supports workforce planning, client retention analysis, and coverage continuity management.']

  Note: 'Master entity tracking the assignment of investment banking coverage bankers to client relationships and industry sectors. Records coverage banker, client entity, sector (TMT, healthcare, energy, financials, industrials), geography, coverage role (primary, secondary, sector specialist), assignment dates, and deal origination attribution. Supports banker accountability, client relationship management, and revenue attribution for the IB division.'
}

Table investment.deal_participant {
  deal_participant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each deal participant record in the investment banking deal association entity. Primary key for the deal_participant table.']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment banking deal (M&A, IPO, DCF, debt/equity capital markets, syndicated lending) to which this participant is associated.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Deal participant jurisdiction is required for sanctions screening, regulatory approval tracking, cross-border deal compliance, and netting enforceability assessment. Banking legal and compliance teams']
  kyc_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.kyc_review. Business justification: Each deal participant requires KYC due diligence before engagement. Compliance tracks which participant record triggered or is associated with each KYC review cycle. Participant onboarding cannot proc']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Deal participants may be internal legal entities in intercompany or cross-border deals; regulatory reporting (CCAR, resolution planning) requires identifying which legal entity each participant belong']
  lei_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lei_registry. Business justification: LEI code required for EMIR/Dodd-Frank trade reporting, counterparty due diligence, and legal entity identification in syndication and capital markets transactions.']
  pledge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collateral.collateral_pledge. Business justification: Deal participants acting as pledgors, secured parties, or custodians are directly associated with specific collateral pledges. Tracking participant_role (pledgor/secured party) against the operative c']
  primary_deal_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the institution or counterparty entity representing the participating organization (e.g., client, co-advisor, legal counsel, auditor, regulator).']
  agreed_fee_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Contractually agreed fee amount payable to or by this participant for their role in the deal, expressed in the deal currency. For success fees, this represents the agreed percentage or fixed amount at deal close.']
  bic_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT Bank Identifier Code (BIC) of the participating institution, used for SWIFT messaging, payment settlement, and cross-border transaction identification in investment banking deal communications.']
  conflict_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the conflict of interest screening was last performed or updated for this participant. Used to ensure checks are current and within required review cycles.']
  conflict_check_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the outcome of the conflict of interest review, including nature of any identified conflict, waiver rationale, or escalation details. Maintained for regulatory audit trail purposes.']
  conflict_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the conflict of interest screening performed for this participant relative to the deal. Values: pending (check not yet initiated), cleared (no conflict found), conflict_identified (potential conflict detected), waived (conflict acknowledged and waived with appropriate approvals), escalated (referred to compliance committee).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this deal participant record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for record origination.']
  deal_team_lead boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this participant is designated as the lead institution or lead advisor for their role in the deal (e.g., lead left bookrunner, lead financial advisor). True = lead; False = co-participant or supporting role.']
  engagement_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant\'s engagement in the deal formally concluded, either through deal close, withdrawal, termination, or completion of advisory mandate. Null if engagement is ongoing.']
  engagement_letter_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal engagement letter was signed between the bank and this participant, establishing the legal basis for the advisory or service relationship within the deal.']
  engagement_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant formally commenced engagement in the deal, typically aligned with the execution of a mandate letter, engagement letter, or non-disclosure agreement (NDA).']
  fee_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Type of fee arrangement agreed with this participant: retainer (periodic fixed fee), success_fee (contingent on deal close), hourly (time-based billing), fixed (lump sum), or no_fee (pro bono or regulatory participant). Drives revenue recognition and deal economics.']
  fee_basis_points decimal(10,4) [note: 'Fee expressed in basis points (BPS) as a proportion of the total deal value, applicable for success fees and underwriting fees in capital markets transactions. One basis point equals 0.01%.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the agreed fee amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency deal fee tracking and FX (Foreign Exchange) conversion.']
  information_barrier_group varchar [note: 'Identifier of the information barrier (Chinese Wall) group to which this participant has been assigned, controlling access to material non-public information (MNPI) about the deal. Used for insider trading prevention and regulatory compliance.']
  institution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of institution participating in the deal: investment_bank, commercial_bank, law_firm, accounting_firm, regulator, private_equity, or hedge_fund. Supports deal team composition analysis and conflict screening. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: investment_bank|commercial_bank|law_firm|accounting_firm|regulator|private_equity|hedge_fund — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  kyc_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent KYC (Know Your Customer) review was completed for this participant. Used to track review currency and trigger periodic refresh cycles per AML compliance requirements.']
  league_table_credit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this participant is eligible to receive league table credit for this deal in industry rankings (e.g., Bloomberg, Dealogic, Thomson Reuters). True = eligible for credit; False = excluded from league table attribution.']
  mandate_letter_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the mandate letter or engagement letter governing this participant\'s involvement in the deal. Links to the formal contractual document establishing scope, fees, and obligations.']
  nda_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) has been executed with this participant prior to sharing confidential deal information. True = NDA in place; False = NDA not yet executed.']
  nda_execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) was formally executed between the bank and this deal participant. Null if NDA has not been executed.']
  participant_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping participant roles into: principal (client, target, acquirer), advisor (financial advisor, legal counsel, auditor, co-advisor), regulatory (regulator, compliance body), or support (escrow agent, trustee, paying agent).']
  participant_ref_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this participant engagement within the deal, used in mandate letters, pitch books, and tombstone records for cross-system identification.']
  participant_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the participant in the investment banking deal. Common roles include: client, target, acquirer, co-advisor, financial advisor, legal counsel, auditor, underwriter, syndicate member, regulator, escrow agent, trustee, fairness opinion provider. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: client|target|acquirer|co_advisor|financial_advisor|legal_counsel|auditor|underwriter|syndicate_member|regulator|escrow_agent|trustee|fairness_opinion_provider — promote to reference product]']
  participant_side varchar [note: 'Indicates which side of the transaction the participant represents: buy_side (acquirer/investor), sell_side (target/issuer), neutral (independent advisor, auditor, fairness opinion), or regulatory (governing body). Critical for conflict of interest screening.']
  participant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the participant\'s engagement in the deal: active (currently engaged), withdrawn (voluntarily exited), terminated (engagement ended by counterparty), completed (deal closed with participant fulfilling role), suspended (engagement paused pending resolution).']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Business email address of the primary contact person at the participating institution. Used for deal communications, document distribution, and regulatory correspondence.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the participating institution responsible for deal communications and execution. Captured for deal team management and correspondence tracking.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number of the primary contact person at the participating institution for deal-related communications.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this participant\'s involvement in the deal requires specific regulatory approval (e.g., antitrust clearance, CFIUS review, FCA approval). True = regulatory approval required; False = no specific approval required.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required regulatory approval for this participant\'s deal involvement: not_required, pending (application submitted), approved (clearance received), rejected (approval denied), or conditional (approved with conditions).']
  syndicate_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of this participant within the deal syndicate or advisory group, where 1 indicates the lead/senior position. Used for tombstone ordering, fee allocation, and league table credit attribution in capital markets transactions.']
  tombstone_credit_line varchar [note: 'The exact credit line text to be used for this participant in the deal tombstone advertisement, specifying the institution name and role as it should appear in the published announcement.']
  tombstone_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this participant\'s involvement should be included in the deal tombstone advertisement published upon deal completion. True = eligible for tombstone credit; False = excluded (e.g., confidential participants, regulatory bodies).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this deal participant record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data lineage, and audit compliance.']
  wall_cross_date date [note: 'Date on which this participant was formally \'wall-crossed\' — i.e., brought over the information barrier and made aware of material non-public information (MNPI) about the deal. Critical for insider trading compliance and information barrier management.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the participant\'s withdrawal or termination from the deal, if applicable. Captures business rationale such as conflict of interest, regulatory objection, commercial disagreement, or deal collapse. Populated only when participant_status is withdrawn or terminated.']

  Note: 'Association entity capturing all parties involved in an investment banking deal — including the client, target company, acquirer, co-advisors, legal counsel, auditors, regulators, and other deal counterparties. Records participant role, institution name, engagement start date, engagement end date, and conflict check status. Enables deal team management and conflict of interest screening.'
}

Table investment.offering {
  offering_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for offering']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Offering pricing_date and settlement_date determine the accounting period for revenue and proceeds recognition. Period-end close requires offerings to be mapped to accounting periods for accurate fina']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Offering proceeds and underwriting fees are tracked against cost centers for capital markets P&L reporting. Underwriting_fee_amount and total_proceeds_amount must be cost-center attributed for managem']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Securities offering proceeds, pricing, and underwriting discount calculations are denominated in a specific currency; prospectus disclosure, regulatory filing, and cross-currency offering analytics re']
  fund_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fund_class. Business justification: Fund share class offerings (e.g., Class A vs. institutional class IPO tranches) have distinct pricing, load structures, and regulatory classifications. Capital markets teams must reference the specifi']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fund. Business justification: Fund IPOs, closed-end fund launches, and ETF offerings require the offering record to reference the issuing fund. Capital markets teams track fund offerings separately from corporate offerings for lea']
  funding_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treasury.funding_plan. Business justification: Bank\'s own debt offerings are planned and tracked in treasury funding plans. Business process: annual funding plan execution includes capital markets issuances to meet funding targets and maturity pro']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.holiday_calendar. Business justification: Offering settlement dates, pricing dates, roadshow scheduling, and lock-up period expiry calculations require holiday calendar validation. Banking capital markets operations use holiday calendars to d']
  instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.instrument. Business justification: Capital markets offerings create tradable securities. Links offering event to resulting instrument for pricing, settlement, prospectus validation, and post-IPO lifecycle management. Essential for regu']
  issuer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate or sovereign entity issuing the securities in this offering. Links to the counterparty/issuer master record.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Securities offerings require jurisdiction reference for regulatory filing type determination (Rule 144A, Reg S, prospectus rules), exchange listing requirements, and securities regulator identificatio']

Business justification: Offerings are issued under specific legal entities for prospectus filing, regulatory approval, and balance sheet booking. The booking legal entity determines regulatory capital treatment and financial']
  listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.listing. Business justification: A public equity or debt offering results in the security being listed on an exchange. Post-IPO/follow-on workflows require linking the offering to the resulting exchange listing for settlement, market']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Offerings are governed by specific regulatory obligations (Securities Act registration requirements, Regulation S, Rule 144A exemption conditions). FK to obligation enables compliance to track which r']
  primary_distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Capital markets offerings are distributed through channels - retail tranches via branches/digital, institutional via RM networks. Syndicate desks track primary distribution channel for allocation deci']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.profit_center. Business justification: Offering revenue (underwriting_fee_amount, total_proceeds_amount) is attributed to profit centers for capital markets P&L reporting. Required for revenue recognition reporting and measuring ECM/DCM de']
  rate_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.rate_benchmark. Business justification: Debt offerings with floating coupon rates reference a specific benchmark rate (SOFR, EURIBOR); IBOR transition disclosure, prospectus documentation, and interest rate risk reporting require linking th']
  reference_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.price. Business justification: Equity and debt offerings are priced relative to the security\'s prevailing market price (bookbuilding, greenshoe exercise, stabilization). Linking offering to security.price records the reference mark']
  regulatory_exam_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_exam. Business justification: SEC and FINRA examiners directly review offerings for registration statement accuracy, prospectus disclosure adequacy, and stabilization compliance. Direct FK from offering to regulatory_exam enables ']
  stress_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.stress_scenario. Business justification: Capital markets offerings are stress-tested for market disruption scenarios affecting pricing, execution risk, and underwriting exposure. Required for risk committee approval and regulatory capital as']
  suitability_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wealth.suitability_assessment. Business justification: MiFID II / SEC Reg BI suitability at offering level: before allocating a capital markets offering to a wealth client, a product-level suitability assessment is required. Linking the offering to the go']
  yield_curve_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.yield_curve. Business justification: Debt offerings (bonds, notes) are priced as a spread to a reference yield curve (e.g., on-the-run Treasury curve, swap curve). Linking offering to security.yield_curve records the pricing benchmark cu']
  bookrunner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead bookrunner (global coordinator) investment bank responsible for managing the bookbuilding process, allocating securities to investors, and stabilizing the aftermarket. For syndicated offerings, this is the primary bookrunner.']
  coupon_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Annual interest rate (coupon) expressed as a percentage of par value for debt offerings (investment grade bonds, high yield bonds, convertible bonds). Null for equity offerings. Key pricing term for DCM transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the offering record was first captured in the investment banking deal management system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping under SOX and SEC requirements.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned to the debt offering by a Nationally Recognized Statistical Rating Organization (NRSRO) such as Moody\'s, S&P, or Fitch at time of issuance (e.g., AAA, Baa2, BB+). Null for unrated or equity offerings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA+|AA|AA-|A+|A|A-|BBB+|BBB|BBB-|BB+|BB|BB-|B+|B|B-|CCC|CC|C|D — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the SEC or relevant regulator declared the registration statement effective, permitting the public offering to proceed. Marks the start of the binding offering period.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the offering record, supporting audit trail requirements and change management tracking across the deal lifecycle.']
  lock_up_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days following the offering closing during which existing shareholders and insiders are contractually restricted from selling their shares. Standard lock-up periods are typically 90 or 180 days for IPOs.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Identifies whether the offering belongs to Equity Capital Markets (ECM) or Debt Capital Markets (DCM), leveraged finance, or structured finance. Drives divisional P&L attribution and regulatory reporting classification.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'Date on which the principal amount of a debt security is due for repayment to bondholders. Applicable to bond and note offerings (investment grade, high yield, convertible). Null for equity offerings.']
  offer_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Final price per share or per unit at which securities are offered to investors, set at pricing date. For debt offerings, expressed as a percentage of par value (e.g., 99.50). Central pricing fact for the ECM/DCM transaction.']
  offering_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned by the investment banking division to uniquely identify this offering across deal management, regulatory filing, and syndication systems (e.g., ECM-2024-00123).']
  offering_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the capital markets offering, tracking progression from deal pipeline through regulatory filing, investor roadshow, pricing, and final closing or withdrawal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pipeline|mandated|filed|roadshow|priced|closed|withdrawn|postponed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  overallotment_amount decimal(22,2) [note: 'Additional securities amount available under the overallotment (greenshoe) option, typically up to 15% of the base offering size. Exercisable by the stabilizing manager to cover excess demand and support post-listing price stability.']
  price_range_high decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper bound of the indicative price range communicated to investors during the roadshow and bookbuilding process, prior to final pricing. Used in preliminary prospectus and investor marketing materials.']
  price_range_low decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower bound of the indicative price range communicated to investors during the roadshow and bookbuilding process, prior to final pricing. Used in preliminary prospectus and investor marketing materials.']
  pricing_date date [note: 'The principal business event date on which the offering price was formally set and agreed between the issuer, underwriters, and investors. Represents the key transaction event timestamp for ECM/DCM deal records.']
  rating_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the Nationally Recognized Statistical Rating Organization (NRSRO) that assigned the credit rating to this debt offering. NR indicates the offering is not rated. Null for equity offerings.']
  reg_s_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offering is eligible for distribution to non-US investors under SEC Regulation S (offshore transactions), enabling international tranche distribution without full SEC registration.']
  roadshow_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the investor roadshow and bookbuilding process concluded, immediately preceding the pricing date. Used to measure roadshow duration and investor engagement analytics.']
  roadshow_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the investor roadshow and bookbuilding process commenced. Marks the beginning of formal investor marketing and demand generation for the offering.']
  rule_144a_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offering qualifies for resale to Qualified Institutional Buyers (QIBs) under SEC Rule 144A, enabling a private placement tranche alongside or instead of a registered public offering.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which securities are delivered to investors and proceeds are remitted to the issuer, typically T+1 or T+2 from pricing date per applicable exchange and regulatory settlement conventions.']
  shares_offered varchar [note: 'Total number of shares or units offered in the transaction (base offering, excluding overallotment). Applicable to equity offerings (IPO, follow-on, rights issue). For debt offerings, represents face value units.']
  size_amount decimal(22,2) [note: 'Total gross proceeds targeted to be raised in the offering, expressed in the offering currency. Represents the base deal size before any overallotment (greenshoe) exercise. Core monetary fact for ECM/DCM transaction records.']
  stabilization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stabilizing manager is authorized to conduct post-listing price stabilization activities (e.g., greenshoe exercise, open market purchases) to support the offering price in the secondary market.']
  syndicate_size varchar [note: 'Total number of investment banks participating in the underwriting syndicate for this offering, including bookrunners, co-managers, and selling group members. Indicates deal complexity and distribution breadth.']
  tombstone_published_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tombstone announcement (deal announcement advertisement) has been published following successful closing of the offering. Tombstones serve as the official public record of completed capital markets transactions.']
  total_proceeds_amount decimal(22,2) [note: 'Actual total gross proceeds raised at closing, including any overallotment option exercised. Represents the final deal size and is the primary monetary outcome metric for the offering.']
  underwriting_discount_bps decimal(8,2) [note: 'Gross underwriting spread expressed in basis points (BPS) as a percentage of total offering proceeds. Represents the total fee paid to the underwriting syndicate, covering management fee, underwriting fee, and selling concession.']
  underwriting_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Absolute monetary value of the gross underwriting fee payable to the underwriting syndicate, derived from the underwriting discount applied to total proceeds. Key revenue metric for investment banking P&L attribution.']
  use_of_proceeds varchar [note: 'Narrative description of how the issuer intends to deploy the capital raised from the offering (e.g., debt repayment, capital expenditure, working capital, acquisitions). Required disclosure in the prospectus under SEC Regulation S-K Item 504.']

  Note: 'Master entity for equity and debt capital markets offerings managed by the investment banking division. Captures offering type (IPO, follow-on, rights issue, convertible bond, investment grade bond, high yield bond, hybrid), issuer, offering size, pricing, currency, exchange listing, regulatory filing status (S-1, F-1, prospectus), settlement date, and ISIN/CUSIP assigned. The primary ECM/DCM transaction record.'
}

Table investment.tranche {
  tranche_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tranche']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Tranche effective_date and maturity_date determine accounting periods for interest accrual, fee amortization, and regulatory capital reporting. Period-end close requires tranches to be mapped to accou']
  benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.benchmark. Business justification: Floating-rate tranches in syndicated loans and structured products reference benchmark indices (SOFR, SONIA, credit index) for coupon calculation and spread pricing. tranche.benchmark_rate is a denorm']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Tranche costs (commitment_fee_rate, underwriting_fee_rate) are tracked against cost centers for deal profitability analysis. Required for management accounting of syndicated loan and bond tranche econ']
  credit_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.credit_rating. Business justification: Structured finance tranches carry independent credit ratings (Moody\'s, S&P, Fitch) used for regulatory capital treatment (Basel risk weights) and investor eligibility screening. tranche.credit_rating,']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Multi-currency syndicated facilities have tranches denominated in different currencies; FX hedging, regulatory capital reporting, and cross-currency interest accrual require a proper currency referenc']
  deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent investment banking deal or transaction that this tranche is part of.']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Multi-tranche deals distribute different tranches through different channels (retail tranche via digital platform, institutional tranche via direct sales desk). Tranche-level distribution channel trac']
  ftp_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treasury.ftp_rate. Business justification: Every loan tranche receives an FTP rate for internal funds transfer pricing and NIM calculation — a core ALM process. Treasury assigns FTP rates to tranches to measure profitability and allocate fundi']
  funding_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treasury.funding_plan. Business justification: Syndicated loan tranches are funded against the bank\'s funding plan — treasury tracks which funding plan supports each tranche for ALM, NSFR compliance, and maturity profile management. No existing FK']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Tranches are posted to specific GL accounts for balance sheet classification (senior debt, subordinated debt, equity instruments). The instrument_type and seniority_level fields signal the need for GL']
  haircut_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collateral.haircut_schedule. Business justification: Each tranche in structured finance has a specific haircut schedule applied to its collateral for regulatory capital treatment and margin calculations. tranche.risk_weight_percent is a denormalized rep']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.holiday_calendar. Business justification: Tranche payment date calculations, first payment date scheduling, and maturity date validation require holiday calendar reference. Banking loan operations teams use holiday calendars to determine vali']
  instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.instrument. Business justification: Structured finance tranches (CLO, ABS, syndicated loan tranches) are themselves tradeable instruments registered in the instrument master. Linking enables pricing, risk capital calculation, and regula']
  interest_rate_risk_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treasury.interest_rate_risk_position. Business justification: Floating-rate and fixed-rate tranches create repricing gaps and duration exposure tracked in IRRBB positions for Basel IRRBB regulatory reporting and ALCO EVE/NII sensitivity analysis. Treasury links ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Tranches are booked under specific legal entities for regulatory capital treatment (regulatory_capital_treatment field) and balance sheet reporting. Legal entity determines RWA calculation and consoli']
  managed_portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wealth.managed_portfolio. Business justification: Private debt / direct lending portfolio tracking: wealth managed portfolios hold specific loan tranches in direct lending and CLO strategies.   obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Tranches are subject to specific regulatory capital obligations (Basel III/IV risk weight treatment, leveraged lending guidelines, Volcker Rule compliance for CLO tranches). tranche.regulatory_capital']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.offering. Business justification: A tranche is a slice of a capital markets offering (e.g., a bond offering with senior and subordinated tranches, or a multi-tranche loan). Linking tranche directly to offering enables reporting on off']
  parent_tranche_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on tranche (parent_tranche_id)']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the financial institution acting as the lead arranger or bookrunner for the tranche.']
  product_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.product_type. Business justification: Tranche instrument type classification drives RWA calculation approach, CECL portfolio segmentation, regulatory capital treatment, and FATCA/CRS reportability.   profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.profit_center. Required for revenue attribution in syndicated lending and capital markets, and for']
  rate_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.rate_benchmark. Business justification: Floating rate tranches reference a specific benchmark rate (SOFR, EURIBOR) for interest calculation; IBOR transition tracking, hedge accounting documentation, and regulatory reporting of benchmark exp']
  regulatory_exam_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_exam. Business justification: Leveraged lending tranches are subject to OCC/Fed leveraged lending guidance examination. Examiners review individual tranches for risk weight accuracy and covenant adequacy. Direct FK enables examine']
  syndication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.investment_syndication. Business justification: In syndicated lending, a facility is divided into tranches (e.g., Term Loan A, Term Loan B, Revolving Credit Facility). Each tranche is part of a specific syndication. Linking tranche to investment_sy']
  transaction_structure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.transaction_structure. Business justification: A transaction structure defines the overall financial and legal architecture of a deal, and tranches are the individual financing components within that structure. Linking tranche to transaction_struc']
  amortization_schedule varchar [note: 'Description or reference to the detailed amortization schedule for the tranche.']
  amortization_type varchar [note: 'The repayment structure of the tranche (e.g., bullet, amortizing, balloon).']
  call_date date [note: 'The earliest date on which the issuer may exercise the call provision.']
  call_provision_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tranche includes a call provision allowing early redemption by the issuer.']
  collateral_description varchar [note: 'Description of the collateral securing the tranche, if applicable.']
  commitment_fee_rate decimal(8,5) [note: 'The commitment fee rate charged on undrawn amounts, expressed as a decimal.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount committed by lenders or underwriters for this tranche.']
  covenant_package varchar [note: 'Classification of the covenant structure attached to the tranche (e.g., covenant-lite, traditional).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tranche record was first created in the system.']
  day_count_convention varchar [note: 'The day count convention used for interest calculation (e.g., 30/360, ACT/360).']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the tranche terms become effective and interest begins accruing.']
  first_payment_date date [note: 'The date of the first scheduled interest or principal payment.']
  interest_rate decimal(8,5) [note: 'The annual interest rate applicable to the tranche, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0525 for 5.25%).']
  interest_rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the interest rate is fixed, floating, or variable.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'The date on which the tranche principal is due for full repayment.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tranche record was last modified.']
  origination_date date [note: 'The date on which the tranche was originated or issued.']
  outstanding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current outstanding principal balance of the tranche.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which interest or principal payments are made.']
  prepayment_penalty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a penalty applies for early prepayment of the tranche.']
  prepayment_penalty_rate decimal(8,5) [note: 'The penalty rate applied to early prepayments, expressed as a decimal.']
  principal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total principal or face value amount of the tranche at origination.']
  regulatory_capital_treatment varchar [note: 'The regulatory capital classification of the tranche under Basel III or other applicable frameworks.']
  security_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the tranche is secured by collateral or unsecured.']
  seniority_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the priority of the tranche in the repayment waterfall relative to other tranches.']
  spread_bps varchar [note: 'The credit spread over the benchmark rate, expressed in basis points.']
  syndication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tranche is syndicated among multiple lenders or underwriters.']
  tranche_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code or identifier for the tranche, used in deal documentation and regulatory filings.']
  tranche_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the tranche, often indicating seniority or class (e.g., Senior Secured, Mezzanine, Class A).']
  tranche_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tranche.']
  tranche_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tranche based on its position in the capital structure and risk profile.']
  underwriting_fee_rate decimal(8,5) [note: 'The underwriting or arrangement fee rate charged for the tranche, expressed as a decimal percentage of principal.']
  utilization_fee_rate decimal(8,5) [note: 'The utilization fee rate applied when drawn amounts exceed a specified threshold, expressed as a decimal.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for tranche. Referenced by tranche_id.'
}

Table wealth.managed_portfolio {
  managed_portfolio_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the managed portfolio. Primary key.']
  benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.benchmark.   next_rebalance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next portfolio rebalancing review.']
  performance_fee_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Performance fee rate expressed as a decimal, typically charged on returns exceeding the benchmark or hurdle rate. Null if no performance fee applies.']

  rebalancing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the portfolio is reviewed and rebalanced to align with target asset allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|event_driven — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'MiFID II client classification determining the level of regulatory protection and disclosure requirements.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which performance and valuation reports are generated and delivered to the client.']
  risk_profile varchar [note: 'Client risk tolerance classification based on suitability assessment, ranging from conservative to aggressive.']
  shariah_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the portfolio adheres to Islamic finance principles and Shariah law.']
  short_selling_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the portfolio is permitted to engage in short selling strategies.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or code of the source system from which this portfolio record originated (e.g., SIMCORP, ALADDIN, LEGACY_WM).']
  tax_status varchar [note: 'Tax treatment classification of the portfolio, affecting investment decisions and reporting requirements.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the portfolio was closed or terminated. Null for active portfolios.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this portfolio record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a discretionary or advisory investment portfolio managed on behalf of a high-net-worth (HNW) or ultra-high-net-worth (UHNW) client. Captures portfolio strategy, benchmark assignment, base currency, AUM, inception date, mandate type (discretionary/advisory), risk profile, investment horizon, fee structure reference, and portfolio status (active/suspended/closed/terminated). Links to client_mandate for contractual terms, investment_policy_statement for investment strategy and pre-mandate proposals, model_portfolio for template allocation and asset allocation targets, and custodian_account for safekeeping. Primary system of record aligned with SimCorp Dimension / BlackRock Aladdin portfolio master.'
}

Suitability assessment and mandate setup require valid currency reference for client constrain']

Business justification: Links IPS benchmark reference to master benchmark data for suitability assessment, performance monitoring, and regulatory compliance (MiFID II suitability). Enables consistent benchmark usage across p']

  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: IPS must be governed under a specific regulatory jurisdiction for MiFID II, SEC, or local suitability rules. Regulatory compliance reporting and audit trails require the governing jurisdiction entity.']
  kyc_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.kyc_review. Business justification: IPS documents inform KYC risk assessments and suitability determinations. Compliance teams review IPS during KYC cycles to validate investment objectives align with customer profile, risk tolerance, a']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: IPS documents are constructed to satisfy specific regulatory obligations (ERISA prudent investor rule, MiFID II suitability obligations, SEC investment adviser fiduciary duty). Compliance officers rev']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client (party) for whom this IPS is established.']
  risk_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_limit. Business justification: The IPS formally defines risk limits (max drawdown, VaR, concentration) that are operationalized as risk_limit records. Linking IPS to risk_limit supports limit governance, IPS compliance monitoring, ']
  stress_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.stress_scenario. Business justification: IPS documents reference specific stress scenarios used to validate that the portfolio strategy remains viable under adverse conditions. IPS approval requires stress scenario validation — a named Inves']
  suitability_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wealth.suitability_assessment. Business justification: An Investment Policy Statement is formally grounded in a suitability assessment — the IPS captures the client\'s risk tolerance, investment objectives, and constraints that are determined by the suitab']
  annual_withdrawal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Expected annual withdrawal amount from the portfolio to meet client spending needs.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the client formally accepted and signed the IPS.']
  concentration_limit_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage of portfolio value that may be invested in a single security or issuer, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the IPS record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the IPS mandate becomes binding and portfolio construction begins.']
  esg_exclusions varchar [note: 'List of industries, sectors, or activities excluded from the portfolio based on ESG criteria (e.g., tobacco, firearms, fossil fuels).']
  esg_preference varchar [note: 'Client\'s preference for ESG integration: none (no ESG consideration), basic (ESG screening), advanced (ESG integration), or impact_focused (mission-driven investing).']
  fee_structure varchar [note: 'Type of fee arrangement: AUM-based (percentage of assets under management), flat fee, performance-based, or hybrid.']
  geographic_restrictions varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or countries excluded from or required in the portfolio (e.g., no emerging markets, US-only, exclude sanctioned countries).']
  investment_objective varchar [note: 'Primary investment goal articulated by the client: capital preservation, income generation, balanced growth, capital appreciation, or aggressive growth.']
  ips_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the IPS document, used for external reference and client communication.']
  ips_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IPS document. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|terminated|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ips_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the IPS lifecycle stage: proposal (pre-acceptance), approved_ips (client-accepted mandate), or amended_ips (revised mandate).']
  ips_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the IPS document, incremented with each amendment or restatement.']
  liquidity_requirement_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the client\'s liquidity needs, including anticipated withdrawals, emergency reserves, and cash flow requirements.']
  management_fee_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual management fee as a percentage of AUM, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.00 for 1%).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal IPS review with the client.']
  performance_fee_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Performance-based fee as a percentage of returns above a hurdle rate, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 20.00 for 20%).']
  prohibited_securities varchar [note: 'Specific securities, issuers, or asset types explicitly prohibited by the client (e.g., employer stock, specific countries, derivatives).']
  proposal_date date [note: 'Date the initial investment proposal was presented to the client.']
  rebalancing_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for portfolio rebalancing: monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, or threshold-based (triggered by deviation).']
  rebalancing_trigger_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage deviation from target asset allocation that triggers a portfolio rebalancing action, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).']
  regulatory_restrictions varchar [note: 'Regulatory or legal constraints applicable to the portfolio, including ERISA fiduciary rules, UCITS limits, or jurisdiction-specific investment restrictions.']
  return_objective_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the client\'s return expectations, including target return rates, benchmarks, and time horizon considerations.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for periodic review and potential amendment of the IPS.']
  risk_capacity varchar [note: 'Client\'s financial ability to absorb investment losses without materially impacting their financial goals.']
  risk_tolerance varchar [note: 'Client\'s stated risk tolerance level, ranging from conservative to aggressive.']
  sector_exclusions varchar [note: 'List of economic sectors excluded from the portfolio per client mandate (e.g., financials, energy, healthcare).']
  target_annual_return_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target annualized return percentage specified in the IPS, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 7.50 for 7.5%).']
  tax_considerations varchar [note: 'Narrative description of tax-related constraints and preferences, including capital gains management, tax-loss harvesting, and jurisdiction-specific tax rules.']
  tax_status varchar [note: 'Tax treatment of the portfolio: taxable, tax-deferred (e.g., IRA, 401k), or tax-exempt (e.g., municipal bonds).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the IPS mandate was terminated or superseded by a new version.']
  time_horizon_years varchar [note: 'Investment time horizon in years, representing the period until the client expects to draw down the portfolio.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the IPS record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Formal Investment Policy Statement (IPS) and pre-mandate investment proposal governing a managed portfolio or client relationship. Captures the full advisory lifecycle from initial proposal (recommended strategy, projected return scenarios, risk metrics, fee illustration, client acceptance status) through approved IPS (asset class ranges, return objectives, risk tolerance, liquidity requirements, time horizon, tax considerations, ESG constraints, prohibited securities, concentration limits, sector exclusions, geographic restrictions, regulatory restrictions, rebalancing triggers, and IPS version history). Serves as the authoritative mandate document for portfolio construction, suitability compliance, and restriction enforcement.'
}

  fund_mandate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fund_mandate. Business justification: Asset allocation compliance monitoring checks each allocation line against the applicable fund mandate constraints (sector limits, geographic restrictions, leverage limits, prohibited asset classes). ']

  model_portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wealth.model_portfolio. The existing FK covers managed_portfolio_id, but there is no FK to model']
  monitoring_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.monitoring_rule. Business justification: Asset allocation drift thresholds and rebalancing triggers are directly governed by compliance monitoring rules (UCITS 5/10/40 concentration rules, ERISA diversification rules). The drift_threshold_pe']
  parent_allocation_asset_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent asset allocation record in a hierarchical allocation structure (nullable for top-level allocations).']
  risk_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_limit. Business justification: Asset allocation target/min/max weight bands are governed by risk limits (concentration limits, asset class limits) in the risk framework. Linking allocation to the enforcing risk_limit supports limit']
  stress_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.stress_scenario. Business justification: Asset allocation models are validated against stress scenarios (equity shock, rate shock) to confirm target weights remain within mandate bounds under adverse conditions — a named Investment Committee']
  actual_weight_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current actual allocation weight as a percentage of total portfolio value, calculated from current holdings and market values.']
  allocation_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of this allocation within the asset class taxonomy (1=top-level asset class, 2=subclass, 3=granular segment).']
  allocation_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or label for this asset allocation strategy (e.g., Conservative Growth, Aggressive Equity).']
  allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or commentary regarding the rationale, constraints, or special considerations for this allocation.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this allocation record (active, inactive, pending approval, under review, archived).']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation approach: strategic (long-term target), tactical (short-term adjustment), dynamic (rules-based rebalancing), or model (template allocation).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this asset allocation strategy was formally approved by the investment committee or authorized party.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the investment committee member, portfolio manager, or relationship manager who approved this allocation strategy.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Primary asset class category for this allocation line (equities, fixed income, alternatives, cash, real estate, commodities).']
  asset_subclass varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification within the asset class (e.g., large-cap equities, investment-grade bonds, private equity, REITs).']
  correlation_coefficient decimal(4,3) [note: 'Statistical correlation of this asset class with the overall portfolio or a reference asset class, ranging from -1.000 to +1.000.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset allocation record was first created in the system.']
  drift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated deviation of actual weight from target weight, expressed as percentage points (actual_weight_percent - target_weight_percent).']
  drift_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable drift from target weight before rebalancing is triggered, expressed as percentage points.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this asset allocation target becomes effective and applicable to the portfolio.']
  esg_alignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether this allocation aligns with Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) investment criteria (True/False).']
  expected_return_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected annualized return for this asset class allocation, used in portfolio optimization and client reporting.']
  expected_volatility_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected annualized standard deviation (volatility) for this asset class allocation, used in risk assessment.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this asset allocation target expires or is superseded by a new allocation strategy (nullable for open-ended allocations).']
  geographic_allocation varchar [note: 'Geographic or regional focus of the allocation (e.g., USA, EUR, GBR, emerging markets, global).']
  last_rebalanced_date date [note: 'Date when this allocation was last rebalanced to bring actual weights back in line with target weights.']
  liquidity_classification varchar [note: 'Liquidity profile of the asset class allocation (highly liquid, liquid, moderately liquid, illiquid) for liquidity risk management.']
  maximum_weight_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable allocation weight as a percentage, defining the upper bound of the rebalancing band.']
  minimum_weight_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable allocation weight as a percentage, defining the lower bound of the rebalancing band.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review of this asset allocation strategy and target weights.']
  rebalancing_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for reviewing and rebalancing this allocation (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annually, annually, event-driven). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annually|annually|event_driven — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rebalancing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether this allocation line has breached drift thresholds and requires rebalancing action (True/False).']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this allocation record, incremented with each update to support change tracking and audit history.']
  risk_tolerance_alignment varchar [note: 'Risk profile category that this allocation is designed to support (conservative, moderate, balanced, growth, aggressive).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this allocation record originated (e.g., SimCorp Dimension, BlackRock Aladdin).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this allocation record in the source system of record.']
  target_weight_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Strategic or tactical target allocation weight as a percentage of total portfolio value (e.g., 60.00 for 60%).']
  tax_efficiency_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the tax efficiency of this asset class allocation (high, medium, low) for tax-aware portfolio management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset allocation record was last modified or updated.']

  Note: 'Strategic and tactical asset allocation targets for a managed portfolio or model portfolio. Records target weights, minimum and maximum bands, actual weights, drift thresholds, asset class hierarchy (equities, fixed income, alternatives, cash), currency allocation, geographic allocation, and effective date. Supports rebalancing workflows and IPS compliance monitoring.'
}

  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the security position was originally acquired or established in the portfolio. Used for holding period determination and tax lot tracking.']
  as_of_date date [note: 'Point-in-time date for which this holding record represents the position. Enables historical position reconstruction and time-series analysis.']
  base_currency_market_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Market value of the holding converted to the portfolio base currency using the FX rate. Used for consolidated portfolio reporting and Assets Under Management (AUM) calculation.']
  cost_basis_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to determine which tax lots are sold first for capital gains calculation. Critical for tax-loss harvesting and capital gains optimization strategies.']
  fx_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate used to convert the security value from its native currency to the portfolio base currency as of the as-of date.']
  holding_period_classification varchar [note: 'Tax classification based on holding period. Short-term applies to positions held one year or less; long-term applies to positions held more than one year. Determines capital gains tax rate.']
  income_type varchar [note: 'Tax classification of income generated by the holding. Determines tax treatment and reporting requirements for client tax documents.']
  investment_objective varchar [note: 'Strategic investment goal associated with this holding within the portfolio strategy. Used for suitability assessment and investment policy statement (IPS) compliance.']
  lot_acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when this specific tax lot was acquired. Used to determine short-term versus long-term capital gains treatment.']
  lot_acquisition_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Per-unit purchase price for this specific tax lot including transaction costs allocated to the lot.']
  lot_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of units in this specific tax lot. Sum of all lot quantities equals the total holding quantity.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tax lot indicating whether it is still held, has been sold, or affected by corporate actions.']
  market_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total market value of the holding calculated as quantity multiplied by market price. Primary input for Assets Under Management (AUM) calculation.']
  pledged_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the holding is pledged as collateral for margin loans, derivatives positions, or other obligations. Critical for available liquidity calculations.']
  portfolio_weight decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage of total portfolio market value represented by this holding. Used for asset allocation analysis and rebalancing decisions.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of units or shares of the security held in the portfolio as of the as-of date. Supports fractional shares for mutual funds and certain securities.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this holding record was first created in the portfolio management system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this holding record was last modified. Supports change tracking and reconciliation processes.']
  restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the holding is subject to trading restrictions such as lock-up periods, regulatory holds, or contractual limitations. Affects liquidity analysis and rebalancing decisions.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent transaction affecting this holding was settled and ownership transferred. Follows T+1 or T+2 settlement conventions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that provided this holding record, such as SimCorp Dimension, BlackRock Aladdin, or custodian feed. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  total_cost_basis decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total acquisition cost of the holding including commissions and fees. Used for capital gains calculation and performance attribution.']
  unit_cost_basis decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average cost per unit of the security for tax and performance calculation purposes. Calculated based on the cost basis method applied to all lots.']
  unrealized_gain_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between market value and total cost basis. Represents paper profit or loss on the holding that has not been realized through sale.']
  unrealized_gain_loss_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Unrealized gain or loss expressed as a percentage of total cost basis. Used for performance reporting and client communication.']
  wash_sale_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this holding is subject to wash sale rules, which disallow loss deductions when substantially identical securities are purchased within 30 days before or after a sale at a loss.']

  Note: 'Point-in-time and current holdings record for each security position within a managed portfolio, including tax lot-level detail. Captures security identifier (ISIN/CUSIP/SEDOL), quantity held, cost basis, market value, MTM price, unrealized gain/loss, weight in portfolio, settlement date, custodian account, as-of date, and lot-level attributes (acquisition date, acquisition price, lot quantity, cost basis method — FIFO/LIFO/specific identification/average cost, short-term vs long-term classification, wash sale flag, lot status). Primary source for AUM calculation, performance attribution, tax-loss harvesting, and capital gains optimization.'
}

Table wealth.portfolio_transaction {
  portfolio_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the portfolio transaction record.']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broker or counterparty through which the transaction was executed.']

Business justification: Portfolio transactions must be attributed to cost centers for management accounting and transaction cost analysis reporting. Business justification: MiFID II best execution reporting and trade reconstruction require linking each portfolio transaction to the authoritative price record at execution time. Role-prefix \'execution_\' distinguishes this f']

Business justification: Digital wealth transactions require session linkage for fraud detection, impersonation investigation, and regulatory audit trails. Linking a portfolio transaction to the authenticated session that ini']
  instruction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_instruction. Business justification: Portfolio cash transactions (subscriptions, redemptions, dividend receipts) generate payment instructions for settlement.   instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the security instrument involved in this transaction.']
  managed_portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the managed portfolio in which this transaction occurred.']

  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the custodian bank holding the securities and processing the settlement.']
  rebalancing_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the portfolio rebalancing event that triggered this transaction, if applicable.']
