  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intercompany transaction indicating the nature of the financial activity between legal entities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: loan|fee|dividend|transfer|recharge|interest|service|royalty|capital_contribution|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transfer_pricing_documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the transfer pricing documentation or study that supports the pricing of this intercompany transaction for tax compliance.']
  transfer_pricing_method varchar [note: 'The transfer pricing methodology applied to determine the arm\'s length price for this intercompany transaction in accordance with tax regulations.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of withholding tax applied to the intercompany transaction in accordance with applicable tax treaties and regulations.']
  withholding_tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage rate of withholding tax applied to the intercompany transaction, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 15.00 for 15%).']

  Note: 'Record of a financial transaction between two legal entities within the banking group requiring intercompany elimination during consolidation. Captures transaction reference, originating legal entity, counterparty legal entity, transaction type (loan, fee, dividend, transfer, recharge), transaction date, accounting period, currency, transaction amount, GL account (originating), GL account (counterparty), elimination status, elimination period, and netting agreement reference. Supports IFRS 10 consolidation and intercompany reconciliation.'
}

Table ledger.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the profit center. Primary key for the profit center entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to ledger.cost_center']
  functional_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Profit centers operate in functional currencies for P&L tracking; this link enables accurate profitability analysis, management reporting, and multi-currency performance measurement in banking operati']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'FK to ledger.legal_entity']
  parent_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to ledger.profit_center']
  alternate_profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Secondary or legacy profit center code used for cross-system mapping, migration tracking, or external reporting. Supports integration with legacy systems and external partners.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annual budgeted profit or loss target for this profit center in the reporting currency. Used for performance measurement and variance analysis.']
  consolidation_group varchar [note: 'The consolidation group or reporting entity to which this profit center\'s financial results are consolidated. Used for group-level financial statement preparation and intercompany elimination.']
  cost_allocation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to allocate costs to this profit center. Direct allocation assigns costs directly incurred. Step-down, reciprocal, and activity-based methods distribute shared costs based on cost drivers and allocation keys.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this profit center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this profit center ceased operations or was closed. Null for currently active profit centers. Used for historical reporting and lifecycle management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this profit center became active and began accumulating financial results. Used for historical reporting and lifecycle management.']
  ftp_rate_assignment varchar [note: 'The FTP methodology assigned to this profit center for internal pricing of funds. Matched maturity assigns rates based on asset/liability duration. Pooled and blended methods use average rates. Critical for accurate profitability measurement in banking.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'The primary geographic region or country where this profit center operates, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Used for geographic segment reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The level of this profit center within the organizational hierarchy. Level 1 represents top-level profit centers, with increasing numbers for deeper levels. Used for hierarchical reporting and aggregation.']
  ifrs_segment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center represents a reportable operating segment under IFRS 8. True for segments that meet the quantitative thresholds or are otherwise disclosed separately in financial statements.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center engages in intercompany transactions that require elimination during consolidation. True for profit centers with significant cross-entity activity.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the individual who last modified this profit center record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this profit center record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']
  lob_classification varchar [note: 'The primary line of business classification for this profit center, aligning with the bank\'s strategic business segments and regulatory reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail_banking|commercial_banking|investment_banking|wealth_management|treasury|capital_markets|asset_management|corporate_trust|payment_services|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique business identifier for the profit center, used in financial reporting and management accounting. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by the finance organization.']
  profit_center_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the profit center\'s business purpose, scope of operations, and strategic objectives. Provides context for financial analysis and reporting.']
  profit_center_name varchar [note: 'The full business name or title of the profit center, used for display and reporting purposes.']
  profit_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the profit center. Active profit centers are operational and accumulate PnL. Inactive or closed profit centers are no longer operational but retained for historical reporting.']
  profit_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the profit center by organizational dimension. Indicates whether the profit center represents a banking segment (retail, commercial, investment banking), product line (loans, deposits, trading), geographic region, business unit, legal entity, or functional area.']
  raroc_target_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The target RAROC rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.1500 for 15%) set for this profit center. Used to evaluate whether the profit center is generating adequate returns relative to the economic capital allocated to it.']
  regulatory_reporting_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the profit center for regulatory capital and risk reporting purposes. Banking book includes traditional lending and deposit activities. Trading book includes market-making and proprietary trading.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'The currency in which this profit center\'s results are reported for consolidation and group reporting purposes, using ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. May differ from functional currency for foreign operations.']
  responsible_executive varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive or senior manager accountable for the profit center\'s financial performance and strategic objectives.']
  revenue_allocation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to allocate revenue to this profit center. Direct allocation assigns revenue from transactions directly attributable to the profit center. Proportional, activity-based, and FTP-based methods distribute shared revenue based on defined allocation keys.']
  rwa_category varchar [note: 'The primary risk category for RWA calculation and capital allocation. Determines how this profit center\'s activities contribute to the bank\'s total risk-weighted assets under Basel III.']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center is subject to SOX financial controls and requires enhanced documentation, segregation of duties, and audit trails. True for profit centers with material financial impact.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the individual who created this profit center record in the system. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Management accounting unit representing a discrete business segment or line of business (LOB) that is measured on profitability, enabling PnL attribution and RAROC reporting. Captures profit center code, profit center name, profit center type (banking segment, product line, geographic region), responsible executive, legal entity, LOB classification, FTP rate assignment, revenue allocation method, cost allocation method, parent profit center, hierarchy level, and active status. Supports segment reporting under IFRS 8 and management accounting.'
}

Table compliance.aml_alert {

  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the financial crime case created if the alert was escalated for deeper investigation beyond Level 1 review.']
  counterparty_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: AML alert risk scoring depends on counterparty country FATF high-risk and OFAC sanctions flags. The existing `counterparty_country_code` is a denormalized plain-text field; normalizing to a FK enables']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: AML alerts track transaction amounts requiring currency reference for regulatory reporting, multi-currency conversion, sanctions screening, and SAR filing. Currency ISO codes, minor units, and convert']
  deposit_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the account associated with the suspicious transaction pattern or behavior that triggered the alert.']
  detection_branch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.branch. Critical for targeted examiner re']
  detection_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: AML alerts must track detection channel (branch, ATM, digital, wire) for BSA/AML regulatory reporting, pattern analysis, and FinCEN SAR filing requirements. Channel attribution is mandatory for regula']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fund. Business justification: AML alerts monitor suspicious activity in fund accounts (e.g., rapid subscriptions/redemptions, structuring). Alerts require fund context for investigation and SAR filing decisions in asset management']
  investor_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.investor_account. Business justification: AML transaction monitoring systems generate alerts on fund investor account activity (e.g., structuring via subscriptions/redemptions). aml_alert already links to deposit_account but not investor_acco']
  kyc_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.kyc_record. Business justification: AML alert analysts require immediate access to the party\'s KYC record (PEP status, risk tier, source of funds, beneficial ownership) to disposition alerts. This is a core AML operations workflow — ale']
  monitoring_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.monitoring_rule. Business justification: AML alerts are generated by transaction monitoring rules. Currently aml_alert stores scenario_id and scenario_name as strings, but these should reference the authoritative monitoring_rule product. Add']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer, account holder, or entity under review that triggered the alert.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'FK to payment.payment_transaction.payment_transaction_id — MUST-HAVE: AML alerts are triggered by suspicious payment transactions. Without this link, investigators cannot trace an alert back to the triggering transaction — a BSA/AML compliance requirement.']
  redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.redemption. Business justification: AML alerts are triggered by suspicious redemption activity (rapid in-and-out patterns, large redemptions to unusual accounts). Linking aml_alert directly to the redemption supports AML investigation w']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_filing. Adding a FK to compliance_regulatory_filing replaces the denormalized filing date with a d']
  sanctions_screening_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening_event. Business justification: AML alerts are frequently triggered by sanctions screening hits (OFAC, UN, EU sanctions lists). Linking the alert to the specific screening event that triggered it provides critical audit trail, enabl']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.subscription. Business justification: AML alerts are triggered by suspicious fund subscription activity (e.g., large cash subscriptions, structuring patterns). aml_alert links to payment_transaction but not to the fund subscription itself']
  adverse_media_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether adverse media or negative news has been identified for the customer or counterparty during the alert review process.']
  alert_narrative varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative generated by the detection system describing the suspicious pattern, transactions, and risk indicators that triggered the alert.']
  alert_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the alert, typically displayed to analysts and used in case management workflows.']
  alert_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the alert in the Level 1 review process (new, assigned to analyst, under review, escalated to case, closed, reopened).']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the alert based on the suspicious pattern detected (e.g., structuring, rapid movement of funds, high-risk geography exposure, unusual activity relative to profile). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: structuring|rapid_movement|high_risk_geography|unusual_activity|layering|smurfing|round_tripping|trade_based_laundering|cash_intensive|funnel_account|dormant_reactivation|sanctions_proximity|other — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the reviewing analyst documenting their investigation findings, rationale for disposition, and any additional context.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date when the alert was assigned to an analyst for review.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the alert was formally closed after disposition and any required follow-up actions were completed.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the analyst for closing the alert as a false positive or true positive without escalation, documenting the rationale for the disposition.']
  counterparty_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external party or beneficiary involved in the suspicious transactions that triggered the alert, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert record was first created in the data warehouse or lakehouse, supporting audit and data lineage requirements.']
  detection_date date [note: 'Date when the alert was generated by the AML transaction monitoring system.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the alert was generated by the detection engine, capturing the exact moment the threshold or rule was breached.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final determination of the alert outcome after analyst review: true positive (suspicious activity confirmed), false positive (benign activity), escalated to case for further investigation, or pending review.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the analyst for escalating the alert to a full case investigation, detailing the suspicious indicators and risk factors identified.']
  high_risk_geography_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether the alert involves transactions with countries or jurisdictions identified as high-risk for money laundering or terrorist financing per FATF or internal risk ratings.']
  lookback_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the historical window analyzed by the detection scenario to identify the suspicious pattern.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the machine learning model or rule set used by the detection engine at the time the alert was generated, supporting model governance and audit trails.']
  pep_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether the customer or beneficial owner associated with the alert is classified as a Politically Exposed Person, requiring enhanced due diligence.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the alert based on risk score, customer risk rating, and scenario severity, guiding the order of analyst review.']
  review_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the analyst completed the review and assigned a disposition to the alert.']
  review_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the analyst began reviewing the alert, marking the start of the investigation workflow.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score assigned by the detection engine indicating the severity or likelihood of money laundering activity, typically on a 0-100 or 0-1000 scale.']
  sar_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether a Suspicious Activity Report was filed with FinCEN as a result of this alert and subsequent investigation.']
  sar_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the Suspicious Activity Report was filed with FinCEN, if applicable.']
  sla_deadline timestamp [note: 'Target date and time by which the alert must be reviewed and dispositioned per internal SLA policies and regulatory requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the AML transaction monitoring platform that generated the alert (e.g., NICE Actimize, Oracle Financial Crime, SAS AML).']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of all transactions associated with the alert, expressed in the account\'s base currency.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of transactions that contributed to the alert generation, indicating the volume of suspicious activity detected.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert record was last modified, capturing changes to status, disposition, or analyst assignments.']

  Note: 'AML transaction monitoring alert generated by the financial crime detection engine (NICE Actimize, Oracle Financial Crime, or equivalent) when a customer transaction pattern, behavior anomaly, or network relationship triggers a detection rule, scenario, or machine learning model threshold. Captures alert type (structuring, rapid movement, high-risk geography, unusual activity), risk score, triggering rule/scenario ID, entity under review (customer, account, counterparty), alert status (new, assigned, under-review, escalated, closed), disposition (true positive, false positive, escalated to case), assigned analyst, assignment date, and SLA deadline. Primary operational record driving the Level 1 alert review workflow in AML transaction monitoring.'
}

Table compliance.aml_case {
  aml_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the AML case. Primary key.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: AML cases aggregate transaction amounts across currencies requiring reference data for regulatory reporting, conversion to base currency for risk scoring, and SAR/CTR filing validation. Currency conve']
  deposit_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary account involved in the suspicious activity.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fund. Business justification: AML investigations often involve funds used for layering/integration schemes. Cases track fund structures exploited for money laundering, requiring fund-level analysis for regulatory reporting and enf']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: AML cases are investigated and reported under jurisdiction-specific laws (BSA/USA PATRIOT Act in US, POCA in UK). The jurisdiction determines SAR filing obligations, law enforcement referral procedure']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: AML cases are investigated within a specific legal entity\'s BSA/AML program. Regulators examine AML programs at the legal entity level. The responsible legal entity determines which regulator oversees']
  originating_branch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.branch. Business justification: AML investigations require branch-level origination tracking for BSA officer assignment, branch-level SAR coordination, and regulatory exam scoping. Existing FK covers originating channel type but not']
  originating_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: AML cases track originating channel for risk assessment, SAR narrative requirements (FinCEN requires channel disclosure), and channel-specific typology detection. Essential for regulatory reporting an']
  primary_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary customer or entity under investigation in this case.']
  regulatory_exam_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_exam. Business justification: aml_case has a regulatory_examination_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the case is subject to regulatory examination. Adding a FK to regulatory_exam replaces the boolean flag with a direct reference to the s']
  alert_count varchar [note: 'Number of AML alerts that were escalated and aggregated into this case.']
  assigned_investigator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the compliance officer or investigator assigned to this case.']
  case_closed_date date [note: 'Date when the AML case investigation was completed and the case was closed.']
  case_closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the AML case investigation was completed and the case was closed.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique case identifier used for regulatory reporting and internal tracking. Format: AAA-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  case_opened_date date [note: 'Date when the AML case was officially opened for investigation.']
  case_opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the AML case was officially opened for investigation.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the AML case investigation.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the financial crime case type being investigated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case record was first created in the system.']
  ctr_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the CTR was filed with FinCEN.']
  ctr_filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Currency Transaction Report must be filed for this case.']
  disposition_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome or resolution of the case investigation.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the case investigation must be completed to meet regulatory timelines.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the case was escalated to senior management or specialized investigation teams.']
  investigation_narrative varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative documenting the investigation findings, analysis, and rationale for case disposition.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the system user who last modified the case record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case record was last modified.']
  law_enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency to which the case was referred.']
  law_enforcement_referral boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case has been referred to law enforcement agencies for further investigation.']
  ofac_screening_result varchar [note: 'Result of OFAC sanctions screening performed on the parties involved in this case.']
  primary_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number of the primary account involved in the suspicious activity.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the case based on risk severity and regulatory urgency.']
  regulatory_examination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case has been flagged for regulatory examination or audit review.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the senior compliance officer or manager who reviewed and approved the case disposition.']
  reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case was reviewed and approved by senior compliance management.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score assigned to the case based on transaction patterns, customer profile, and alert severity. Scale 0-100.']
  sanctions_list_match varchar [note: 'Name of the sanctions list (e.g., SDN, EU Sanctions) if a match was identified during screening.']
  sar_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the SAR was filed with FinCEN.']
  sar_filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Suspicious Activity Report must be filed with FinCEN for this case.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the AML transaction monitoring or case management system that originated this case (e.g., NICE Actimize, Oracle Financial Crime).']
  total_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of all transactions under investigation in this case.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of transactions under review in this case.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the system user who created the case record.']

  Note: 'Financial crime investigation case opened when one or more AML alerts are escalated for deeper review. Tracks case type (AML, fraud, sanctions), assigned investigator, case status, priority, linked entities (customers, accounts, transactions), investigation narrative, disposition outcome, and regulatory filing obligations. Serves as the primary case management record aligned with NICE Actimize Case Manager.'
}

  source_of_wealth_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the source of wealth has been verified and documented. Typically required for high-risk and EDD customers.']

  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the document verification was completed.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the authenticity of the document: manual (visual inspection), electronic (e-verification), third_party (external service), biometric, notarized, or certified_copy.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of document verification: verified (passed), pending (awaiting verification), failed (could not verify), expired (document expired), rejected (document not acceptable), or not_required.']

  Note: 'Know Your Customer (KYC) / Customer Due Diligence (CDD) periodic review record capturing the review cycle, due diligence level (SDD/CDD/EDD), review trigger (periodic, event-driven, risk-based), assigned analyst, completion date, risk rating outcome, and approval workflow state. Includes documentary evidence collected during the review: document type (passport, utility bill, articles of incorporation, audited financials), document reference number, issuing authority, issue/expiry dates, verification method, and verification status. Tracks the full lifecycle of each KYC refresh event including documentation completeness. Authoritative record for all CDD/EDD review activity and supporting documentation.'
}

Table compliance.sanctions_screening_event {
  sanctions_screening_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sanctions screening event record.']
  aml_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.aml_case. Business justification: A sanctions screening event with a confirmed match (match_status = confirmed, blocking_action_taken = true) typically escalates to an AML case for full investigation. Linking sanctions_screening_event']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fund. Business justification: Funds (especially offshore/alternative structures) are screened against sanctions lists. Screening events track fund-level matches requiring blocking actions, regulatory reporting, and investment rest']
  monitoring_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific screening rule or algorithm that triggered the match.']
  primary_sanctions_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity being screened (customer, counterparty, payment beneficiary, correspondent bank).']
  sanctions_list_entry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the specific watchlist entry that matched.']

Screened_entity_id(unlinked) represents various entity types; explicit account FK enables account screening event queries for O']
  screened_entity_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Sanctions screening requires knowing the screened entity\'s country to apply the correct sanctions program (OFAC, EU, UN). Country-level FATF high-risk and OFAC sanctions flags directly determine scree']

Business justification: Sanctions screening events triggered at a branch (e.g., teller-initiated wire transfer) require branch-level attribution for BSA officer review, branch exam scoping, and FinCEN reporting. Existing FK ']
  screening_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Sanctions screening events track channel context (payment channel, account opening channel, wire transfer) for OFAC reporting, blocking action implementation, and channel-specific screening rule confi']
  screening_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Sanctions screening is conducted under specific jurisdictional sanctions regimes (OFAC for US, EU Consolidated List, UN Security Council). The screening_jurisdiction_id determines which sanctions prog']
  analyst_review_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes and rationale documented by the compliance analyst during review of the sanctions match.']
  analyst_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the screening result requires manual analyst review.']
  analyst_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the compliance analyst completed their review of the screening event.']
  blocking_action_taken boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a blocking action was taken on the transaction or relationship due to the sanctions match.']
  blocking_action_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of blocking action taken in response to the sanctions match.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sanctions screening event record was first created in the system.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition or outcome of the sanctions screening event after analyst review.']

  false_positive_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sanctions match was determined to be a false positive after investigation.']
  match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical confidence score of the sanctions match (0-100 scale).']
  match_status varchar [note: 'Overall result of the sanctions screening indicating whether a match was found.']
  match_type varchar [note: 'Type of match algorithm that identified the potential sanctions hit.']
  matched_entity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity on the sanctions watchlist that matched the screened entity.']
  matched_list_type varchar [note: 'Category of the watchlist where the match was identified.']
  ofac_report_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a blocking report was filed with OFAC for this sanctions match.']
  ofac_report_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the OFAC blocking report was filed.']
  regulatory_hold_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory hold was placed on the entity or transaction due to the sanctions match.']
  regulatory_hold_details varchar [note: 'Detailed information about the regulatory hold including scope, duration, and legal basis.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the sanctions screening event based on match quality and entity profile.']
  sar_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Suspicious Activity Report was filed with FinCEN as a result of this screening event.']
  sar_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the Suspicious Activity Report was filed with FinCEN.']

  screened_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity being screened for sanctions compliance.']

  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sanctions screening was performed.']
  screening_trigger varchar [note: 'Business event that triggered the sanctions screening.']
  screening_type varchar [note: 'Type of screening workflow executed (real-time payment screening, batch customer screening, event-driven screening).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sanctions screening event record was last modified.']
  watchlist_consulted varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sanctions watchlists consulted during screening (OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated, UN Security Council, HMT, internal high-risk).']
  watchlist_last_refresh_date date [note: 'Date when the sanctions watchlist was last updated or refreshed.']
  watchlist_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the sanctions watchlist used during screening.']

  Note: 'OFAC and international sanctions screening event record capturing the screened entity (customer, counterparty, payment beneficiary, correspondent bank), screening list(s) consulted (OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated, UN Security Council, HMT, internal high-risk), match results including match score, match type (exact, fuzzy, alias), match status (no-match, potential-match, confirmed-match), blocking action taken, disposition, analyst review notes, escalation status, regulatory hold details, and screening timestamp. Includes watchlist reference metadata (list name, type, issuing authority, version, last refresh date) and detailed match artifacts (matched entity name, matched list entry, match confidence score, blocking action, escalation status). Supports real-time payment screening, batch customer screening, and event-driven screening workflows. Authoritative record for all screening events, match results, and watchlist references.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_exam {
  regulatory_exam_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory examination record. Primary key for the regulatory exam entity.']
  examined_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Regulatory exams often scope specific channels (branch network BSA exam, digital banking security exam, ATM ADA compliance). Exam findings and MRAs cite channel scope for targeted remediation and foll']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Regulatory exams are jurisdiction-specific (Federal Reserve, OCC, state banking departments); jurisdiction reference provides regulatory framework, examination authority, enforcement powers, and appli']
  asset_quality_rating varchar [note: 'The CAMELS component rating for asset quality. Evaluates credit risk, loan portfolio quality, and adequacy of loan loss reserves. Scale of 1 (strongest) to 5 (weakest).']
  capital_adequacy_rating varchar [note: 'The CAMELS component rating for capital adequacy. Assesses the quantity and quality of capital relative to risk profile. Scale of 1 (strongest) to 5 (weakest).']
  composite_rating varchar [note: 'The overall composite rating assigned to the institution based on the CAMELS rating system (Capital adequacy, Asset quality, Management, Earnings, Liquidity, Sensitivity to market risk). Scale of 1 (strongest) to 5 (weakest).']
  confidential_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the examination report and findings are subject to confidential supervisory information restrictions and cannot be publicly disclosed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory examination record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  earnings_rating varchar [note: 'The CAMELS component rating for earnings performance. Evaluates profitability, earnings quality, and sustainability of earnings. Scale of 1 (strongest) to 5 (weakest).']
  enforcement_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the examination resulted in or is associated with a formal enforcement action such as a consent order, cease and desist order, or civil money penalty.']
  exam_methodology varchar [note: 'The approach or format used to conduct the examination. Specifies whether examiners conducted the review on-site at the institution, remotely off-site, or using a hybrid approach.']
  exam_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional notes, observations, or context related to the examination engagement that do not fit into structured fields.']
  exam_number varchar [note: 'The official examination number or reference code assigned by the regulatory agency. This is the externally-known identifier used in all regulatory correspondence and documentation.']
  exam_period_end_date date [note: 'The ending date of the period under examination. Defines the end of the lookback window for transactions, controls, and activities being reviewed.']
  exam_period_start_date date [note: 'The beginning date of the period under examination. Defines the start of the lookback window for transactions, controls, and activities being reviewed.']
  exam_priority varchar [note: 'The priority classification of the examination. Indicates whether this is a routine supervisory cycle exam, a targeted review of specific risks, a for-cause examination due to concerns, or a follow-up to prior findings.']
  exam_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the examination scope, including specific business lines, processes, systems, or risk areas to be reviewed. Defines the boundaries and focus areas for the examination engagement.']
  exam_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the regulatory examination. Tracks the examination from initial scheduling through final closure and response completion.']
  exam_team_size varchar [note: 'The total number of examiners assigned to the examination engagement. Indicates the scale and resource commitment of the regulatory review.']
  exam_type varchar [note: 'The category or focus area of the regulatory examination. Defines the primary scope and regulatory framework under which the examination is conducted.']
  exit_meeting_date date [note: 'The date of the formal exit meeting or closing conference between examiners and institution management. Represents the date when preliminary findings were discussed.']
  fieldwork_end_date date [note: 'The date when on-site or remote examination fieldwork activities concluded. Marks the completion of active data gathering and testing by examiners.']
  fieldwork_start_date date [note: 'The date when on-site or remote examination fieldwork activities commenced. Marks the beginning of active examiner engagement with the institution.']
  findings_count varchar [note: 'The total number of examination findings, matters requiring attention, or deficiencies identified during the examination. Quantifies the volume of issues discovered.']
  internal_coordinator_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the internal bank coordinator responsible for managing examination logistics and information requests.']
  internal_coordinator_name varchar [note: 'The name of the internal bank employee or officer designated as the primary coordinator and liaison for the examination engagement.']
  lead_examiner_email varchar [note: 'The official email address of the lead examiner for examination-related correspondence and communication.']
  lead_examiner_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the lead examiner or examiner-in-charge responsible for overseeing the examination engagement. Primary point of contact from the regulatory agency.']
  lead_examiner_phone varchar [note: 'The direct phone number of the lead examiner for examination-related inquiries and coordination.']
  liquidity_rating varchar [note: 'The CAMELS component rating for liquidity position. Assesses funding stability, liquidity risk management, and ability to meet obligations. Scale of 1 (strongest) to 5 (weakest).']
  management_rating varchar [note: 'The CAMELS component rating for management capability. Assesses board and management oversight, risk management practices, and compliance culture. Scale of 1 (strongest) to 5 (weakest).']
  matters_requiring_attention_count varchar [note: 'The number of Matters Requiring Attention (MRA) or Matters Requiring Board Attention (MRBA) formally cited in the examination report. Represents significant supervisory concerns requiring corrective action.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this regulatory examination record. Audit trail for accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory examination record was last modified or updated. Audit trail for record changes.']
  report_issue_date date [note: 'The date the final examination report was officially issued by the regulatory agency. Marks the formal delivery of findings and recommendations to the institution.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the institution must submit its formal written response to examination findings and proposed corrective action plans.']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'The date the institution submitted its formal written response to the examination findings and corrective action plans to the regulatory agency.']
  sensitivity_rating varchar [note: 'The CAMELS component rating for sensitivity to market risk. Evaluates exposure to interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, and other market risks. Scale of 1 (strongest) to 5 (weakest).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this regulatory examination record. Audit trail for accountability.']

  Note: 'Regulatory examination or supervisory review record initiated by a governing body (Fed, OCC, FDIC, CFPB, FINRA). Captures examiner agency, examination type (safety and soundness, BSA/AML, consumer compliance, capital adequacy), examination period, scope, lead examiner, status, findings count, and exit meeting date. Master record for managing regulatory examination engagements.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_filing {
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory filing record. Primary key for the compliance regulatory filing entity.']
  filing_branch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.branch. Business justification: CTR and SAR filings require conducting location (branch) as mandatory FinCEN BSA E-Filing field. Branch attribution enables filing accuracy validation, branch-level BSA program assessment, and regulat']
  filing_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Regulatory filings (SARs, CTRs, FATCA, CRS) are submitted to specific jurisdictional regulators (FinCEN, FCA, BaFin). The filing_jurisdiction_id links each filing to its governing jurisdiction, enabli']
  filing_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Regulatory filings (FATCA, CRS, FR Y-9C, call reports) are submitted by specific legal entities. The filing legal entity is a required regulatory field. Role-prefixed to distinguish from subject_party']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fund. Business justification: FATCA, CRS, AIFMD Annex IV, and UCITS regulatory filings are made at the fund level. compliance_regulatory_filing has no existing link to fund — a critical gap for investment fund regulatory reporting']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: A regulatory filing is made to fulfill a specific regulatory obligation (e.g., FATCA reporting obligation, BSA CTR filing obligation, SAR filing obligation). Linking compliance_regulatory_filing to ob']
  regulatory_exam_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_exam. Business justification: Regulatory filings are often submitted in direct response to or in the context of a regulatory examination (e.g., FATCA/CRS reports requested during an OCC exam). Linking compliance_regulatory_filing ']
  subject_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: SARs, CTRs, and FATCA filings must identify the specific customer party involved. Regulatory reporting requires structured party linkage for FinCEN submissions and audit validation. Removes denormaliz']
  transaction_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Regulatory filings (FATCA, CRS, CTR) report transaction amounts in specific currencies; the `transaction_currency_code` is a denormalized plain-text field. Normalizing to a FK enables currency-specifi']
  account_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Account balance or value as of the end of the FATCA or CRS reporting year. This is the total value of financial assets held in the account, including deposits, securities, and other investments.']
  account_holder_tin varchar [note: 'Tax identification number of the account holder for FATCA or CRS reporting. For U.S. persons, this is the Social Security Number (SSN) or Employer Identification Number (EIN). For foreign persons, this is the foreign tax identification number.']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory agency acknowledged receipt of the filing. For FinCEN filings, this is the date the BSA E-Filing System confirms successful submission.']
  actual_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the filing was actually submitted to the regulatory agency. Used to track timeliness and compliance with submission deadlines.']
  amendment_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this filing is an amendment or correction to a previously submitted filing. True = this is an amended filing, False = this is an original filing.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory filing was approved for submission by the reviewer or compliance officer. Marks the transition from draft to approved status.']
  conducting_party_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the conducting party, such as driver\'s license number, passport number, or other government-issued identification. Required for CTR filings to verify the identity of the person conducting the transaction.']
  conducting_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or entity that conducted the transaction reported in a CTR filing. This is the person who physically presented the currency or initiated the transaction.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this filing contains confidential or sensitive information that requires special handling and access controls. True = confidential, False = standard handling. SAR filings are always confidential by law.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory filing record was first created in the system. Marks the beginning of the filing lifecycle and is used for audit trail and compliance tracking.']
  exemption_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction or customer is exempt from CTR reporting requirements under FinCEN exemption rules. Phase I exemptions apply to banks and government entities. Phase II exemptions apply to certain non-listed businesses and payroll customers.']
  fatca_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the jurisdiction to which FATCA or CRS reporting is submitted. For FATCA, this is typically the jurisdiction of the foreign financial institution. For CRS, this is the jurisdiction of the account holder\'s tax residency.']
  fatca_reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which FATCA or CRS reporting is being submitted. FATCA requires annual reporting of financial accounts held by U.S. persons at foreign financial institutions, and CRS requires reporting of accounts held by foreign tax residents.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory filing. Draft = initial preparation, Pending Review = awaiting internal approval, Approved = cleared for submission, Submitted = sent to regulatory agency, Acknowledged = receipt confirmed by agency, Rejected = returned by agency for correction, Amended = corrected version submitted, Withdrawn = filing cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Pending Review|Approved|Submitted|Acknowledged|Rejected|Amended|Withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fincen_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking number assigned by FinCEN upon successful submission of a BSA filing (SAR, CTR, FBAR, Form 8300). This number is used for all subsequent correspondence and inquiries regarding the filing.']
  income_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total income paid or credited to the account during the FATCA or CRS reporting year. Includes interest, dividends, gross proceeds from sales, and other income types as defined by FATCA and CRS regulations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory filing record was last updated or modified. Used to track changes and maintain audit trail for compliance and internal control purposes.']
  preparer_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the preparer for follow-up inquiries by the regulatory agency. Required on many regulatory filings to facilitate communication.']
  preparer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the compliance officer, analyst, or staff member who prepared the regulatory filing. Used for accountability, audit trail, and quality control purposes.']
  prior_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or tracking identifier of the original filing that this amendment or correction is replacing. Required when amendment_indicator is True.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Governing body or regulatory authority to which the filing is submitted. FinCEN = Financial Crimes Enforcement Network, Fed = Federal Reserve System, OCC = Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, FDIC = Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, SEC = Securities and Exchange Commission, FINRA = Financial Industry Regulatory Authority, CFTC = Commodity Futures Trading Commission, CFPB = Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, IRS = Internal Revenue Service, EBA = European Banking Authority, PRA = Prudential Regulation Authority. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FinCEN|Fed|OCC|FDIC|SEC|FINRA|CFTC|CFPB|IRS|EBA|PRA — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period covered by this regulatory filing. For periodic filings, this is the last day of the reporting quarter or year. For event-driven filings, this may represent the end of the activity window or the date of the reportable event.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by this regulatory filing. For periodic filings (Call Report, FR Y-9C), this is the beginning of the reporting quarter or year. For event-driven filings (SAR, CTR), this may represent the start of the activity window.']
  retention_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory filing record is eligible for archival or deletion based on regulatory retention requirements. BSA records must be retained for 5 years from the date of filing. Other filings may have different retention periods.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the compliance manager or senior officer who reviewed and approved the regulatory filing before submission. Part of the internal control and approval workflow.']
  submission_deadline_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the filing must be submitted to the governing body. Failure to meet this deadline may result in penalties, fines, or enforcement actions.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Technical method or channel used to submit the filing to the regulatory agency. BSA E-Filing = FinCEN\'s electronic filing system, FRB E-Filing = Federal Reserve electronic submission, SEC EDGAR = SEC\'s Electronic Data Gathering Analysis and Retrieval system, FINRA Gateway = FINRA\'s submission portal, Manual Upload = web-based manual submission, API = application programming interface, SFTP = secure file transfer protocol. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BSA E-Filing|FRB E-Filing|SEC EDGAR|FINRA Gateway|Manual Upload|API|SFTP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  suspicious_activity_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount involved in the suspicious activity reported in a SAR filing. Represents the cumulative value of transactions or account activity that triggered the filing.']
  suspicious_activity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the suspicious activity, including facts, circumstances, and reasons for suspicion. This is the free-text explanation provided in Part V of the SAR form and is critical for law enforcement review.']
  suspicious_activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of suspicious activity reported in a SAR filing. Examples include structuring, money laundering, terrorist financing, fraud, identity theft, elder financial exploitation, cyber event, or other financial crimes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Structuring|Money Laundering|Terrorist Financing|Fraud|Identity Theft|Elder Financial Exploitation|Cyber Event|Bribery/Corruption|Embezzlement|Check Fraud|Wire Fraud|ACH Fraud|Credit Card Fraud|Mortgage Loan Fraud|Consumer Loan Fraud|Trade-Based Money Laundering|Bulk Cash Smuggling|Funnel Account|Unlicensed Money Services Business|Other — promote to reference product]']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the specific transaction reported in a CTR filing. For CTR, this is the total amount of currency (cash) involved in the transaction that exceeded the $10,000 reporting threshold.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the specific transaction or event that triggered the regulatory filing. For CTR filings, this is the date of the currency transaction. For SAR filings, this may be the date of the initial suspicious transaction or the date the activity was detected.']

  Note: 'Master record for all periodic and event-driven regulatory filings submitted to governing bodies (FinCEN, Fed, OCC, FDIC, SEC, FINRA, CFTC, IRS). Captures filing type (SAR, CTR, Call Report, FR Y-9C, CCAR, DFAST, Form PF, TIC, FATCA/CRS), regulatory agency, reporting period, submission deadline, actual submission date, filing status, and submission reference number. For BSA filings (SAR): subject entity, activity type, suspicious activity description, dollar amount, FinCEN tracking number, and underlying case reference. For BSA filings (CTR): transaction date, amount, currency, conducting party, branch location, exemption status, and FinCEN acknowledgment number. For FATCA/CRS: reporting year, jurisdiction, account holder tax identification, account balance, and income amounts. Single authoritative registry of all regulatory submissions with type-discriminated attributes.'
}

Table compliance.obligation {
  obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for obligation']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fund. Business justification: Regulatory obligations (UCITS diversification rules, AIFMD leverage limits, FATCA fund registration) are scoped to specific funds. obligation has jurisdiction_id but no fund link — a gap preventing fu']
  fund_mandate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fund_mandate. Business justification: Regulatory obligations are implemented through fund mandates — e.g., UCITS Article 52 concentration limits are reflected as fund mandate constraints. Linking obligation to fund_mandate supports policy']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Compliance obligations are jurisdiction-specific (Basel III in EU vs US, MiFID2 in EEA, Dodd-Frank in US); jurisdiction reference provides regulatory framework, implementation timeline, and applicabil']
  regulatory_exam_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_exam. Business justification: obligation has last_regulatory_exam_date (DATE) and exam_findings (STRING) indicating the obligation has been reviewed during a regulatory examination. Adding a FK to regulatory_exam replaces the deno']
  superseded_by_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the obligation ID that supersedes or replaces this obligation, if applicable. Used to track regulatory changes and obligation evolution. Null if not superseded.']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Textual description of the scope of applicability within the bank: which lines of business (LOB), product types, geographies, or legal entities this obligation applies to (e.g., \'All retail banking operations in USA\', \'Investment banking M&A advisory\', \'All deposit accounts\').']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'The degree to which compliance monitoring and reporting for this obligation is automated: fully automated (no manual intervention), partially automated (some manual steps), manual (entirely manual process), or hybrid (combination of automated and manual).']
  compliance_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which compliance with this obligation must be demonstrated or reported: continuous monitoring, daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, ad-hoc, or event-driven. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continuous|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|ad_hoc|event_driven — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current assessment of the bank\'s compliance with this obligation: compliant (fully meeting requirements), non-compliant (failing to meet requirements), partially compliant (meeting some but not all requirements), not assessed (no recent assessment), in remediation (actively addressing gaps), or exempt (granted exemption or waiver).']
  control_objective varchar [note: 'The high-level control objective or purpose this obligation is designed to achieve (e.g., \'Prevent money laundering and terrorist financing\', \'Ensure capital adequacy\', \'Protect customer data privacy\', \'Maintain accurate financial reporting\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this obligation record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this obligation becomes legally binding and enforceable. The bank must be compliant from this date forward.']
  escalation_procedure varchar [note: 'Description of the escalation path and procedures to follow when non-compliance is identified or when issues arise (e.g., \'Escalate to Chief Compliance Officer within 24 hours\', \'Notify Board Risk Committee for critical violations\').']
  evidence_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of the documentation, records, or evidence that must be maintained to demonstrate compliance with this obligation (e.g., \'Transaction monitoring reports\', \'SAR filing confirmations\', \'Board meeting minutes\', \'Audit trails\').']
  exam_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of findings, observations, or matters requiring attention (MRA) from the most recent regulatory examination related to this obligation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this obligation ceases to be applicable or is superseded. Null for obligations with no defined end date.']
  external_audit_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this obligation is subject to external audit or regulatory examination (True) or is only internally monitored (False).']
  implementation_guidance varchar [note: 'Summary of implementation guidance, best practices, or internal procedures for meeting this obligation. May reference internal policy documents or control procedures.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent compliance assessment or audit for this obligation.']
  last_regulatory_exam_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent regulatory examination or supervisory review that covered this obligation.']
  next_assessment_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next compliance assessment or review of this obligation.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code or reference number for the obligation, used for external identification and regulatory mapping (e.g., BSA-AML-001, FATCA-2023-05).']
  obligation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the obligation, including the specific requirement, scope, and what the bank must do to comply.']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the obligation: active (in force), inactive (not currently applicable), pending (awaiting effective date), superseded (replaced by newer obligation), under review (being assessed), or retired (no longer applicable).']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the obligation by its nature: regulatory reporting (e.g., CCAR, DFAST), operational control (e.g., SOX control), policy requirement, supervisory guidance, legal mandate, or contractual obligation.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business unit, department, or individual responsible for ensuring compliance with this obligation (e.g., \'Chief Compliance Officer\', \'AML Compliance Team\', \'Risk Management Division\').']
  owner_email varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for the obligation owner or responsible party.']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Description of potential penalties, fines, sanctions, or consequences for non-compliance with this obligation (e.g., \'Civil monetary penalties up to $1M per violation\', \'Regulatory enforcement action\', \'License suspension\').']
  product_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product types to which this obligation applies (e.g., \'Demand Deposit Accounts\', \'Mortgage Loans\', \'Derivatives\', \'Securities Trading\'). Null if applicable to all products.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which remediation activities must be completed to achieve full compliance. Null if no remediation is required.']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Summary of the current remediation plan or corrective actions being taken to address any compliance gaps. Null if fully compliant.']
  reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of any regulatory reporting obligations associated with this requirement (e.g., \'Quarterly CCAR submission\', \'Annual FATCA reporting\', \'SAR filing within 30 days of detection\').']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'The assessed risk level associated with non-compliance with this obligation: critical (severe regulatory or financial impact), high (significant impact), medium (moderate impact), or low (minimal impact).']
  system_of_record varchar [note: 'The primary operational system or platform used to manage compliance with this obligation (e.g., \'NICE Actimize\', \'AxiomSL\', \'Oracle Financial Crime\', \'Internal GRC Platform\').']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title or name of the regulatory obligation (e.g., \'Customer Due Diligence Requirements\', \'Suspicious Activity Reporting\').']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last updated this obligation record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this obligation record was last modified or updated.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this obligation record.']

  Note: 'Master record for a specific regulatory or legal obligation the bank must comply with, derived from laws, regulations, rules, or supervisory guidance (e.g., BSA, Dodd-Frank, GDPR, FATCA, Basel III). Captures obligation source, regulatory body, effective date, applicability scope (LOB, geography, product type), obligation owner, and compliance status. Serves as the regulatory obligation inventory underpinning the GRC framework.'
}

Business justification: Currency Transaction Reports (CTRs) may be associated with AML investigations when large cash transactions are part of suspicious activity patterns.   filing_branch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.branch. Branch attribution enables filing accuracy validation, branch-level CTR compliance m']

The filing_jurisdiction_id identifies the regulatory authority receiving the CTR, ']
  filing_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Role-prefixed to distinguish from party_id. No existing un']
  monitoring_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.monitoring_rule. Business justification: CTR filings are triggered by AML/BSA monitoring rules that detect cash transactions exceeding $10,000 thresholds. Linking ctr_filing to monitoring_rule establishes which rule configuration triggered t']

  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date on which FinCEN acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the CTR filing.']
  aggregated_transactions_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CTR represents multiple transactions aggregated to exceed the $10,000 threshold.']

  multiple_persons_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether multiple persons were involved in conducting the reportable transaction(s).']
