  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of cash transaction that triggered the CTR filing requirement.']

  Note: 'Currency Transaction Report (CTR) filing record for cash transactions exceeding $10,000 as required by BSA. Captures transaction details, filing date, FinCEN BSA ID, and filing status.'
}

Table compliance.policy {
  policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance policy record. Primary key.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Policies are jurisdiction-specific (US privacy policy vs EU GDPR policy); jurisdiction reference provides applicability scope, regulatory framework, and enforcement context. Essential for policy gover']
  primary_superseded_by_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the policy that replaces this one, if applicable. Null if the policy is current.']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the organizational units, lines of business (LOB), or employee populations to which this policy applies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the policy was formally approved by the designated governance authority.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the policy within the governance workflow.']
  attestation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees must formally attest to their understanding and compliance with this policy.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level governing access and distribution of the policy document.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the policy record was first created in the system.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the full policy document stored in the document management system or intranet.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the policy becomes enforceable and operational within the organization.']
  enforcement_level varchar [note: 'Degree of enforceability and consequences for non-adherence to the policy.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the policy is scheduled to expire or be reviewed for renewal. Null indicates no scheduled expiration.']
  external_audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy is subject to external audit review and validation.']
  governance_committee varchar [note: 'Name of the board committee or governance body responsible for oversight of this policy (e.g., Asset-Liability Committee (ALCO), Risk Committee, Audit Committee).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the policy record was most recently updated.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the policy was most recently reviewed and assessed for accuracy and compliance.']
  lob_applicability varchar [note: 'Specific lines of business covered by this policy (e.g., Retail Banking, Investment Banking, Wealth Management, Treasury).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of this policy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, clarifications, or context regarding the policy implementation or interpretation.']
  policy_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory domain or compliance area that this policy addresses, aligned with industry-standard frameworks and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aml|kyc|sanctions|sox|fatca|crs|bsa|ccar|dfast|ifrs9|cecl|frtb — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of the policy objectives, scope, and key requirements it implements.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the compliance policy as it appears in official documentation and regulatory filings.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the compliance policy, externally referenced in regulatory documentation and internal governance materials.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the policy indicating its operational state and enforceability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_review|approved|active|suspended|retired|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the policy based on its primary purpose and scope within the compliance framework.']
  public_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy or its summary must be publicly disclosed per regulatory requirements.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'The governing authority or regulatory agency that issued the underlying requirement (e.g., Federal Reserve, SEC, OCC, FINRA).']
  regulatory_examination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy has been reviewed or cited during regulatory examinations.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which the policy must be reviewed and reaffirmed to ensure continued relevance and compliance.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the inherent risk level associated with non-compliance with this policy.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short title or abbreviated name used for internal reference and reporting.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees subject to this policy must complete mandatory training.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the policy document, following semantic versioning conventions.']

  Note: 'Compliance policy and procedure master record defining the banks internal policies implementing regulatory requirements. Captures policy name, effective date, review cycle, owner, and approval status.'
}

Table compliance.monitoring_rule {
  monitoring_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the AML/BSA transaction monitoring rule configuration record. Primary key.']
  applicable_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: AML monitoring rules scope applicable channels (wire transfer rules don\'t apply to ATM, structuring rules target branch/teller) for scenario configuration, alert generation logic, and false positive r']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fund. Business justification: AML monitoring rules are calibrated per fund type (hedge fund vs mutual fund). Rules detect fund-specific typologies (late trading, market timing), requiring fund linkage for rule tuning and alert inv']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: AML/BSA monitoring rules are technical implementations of specific regulatory obligations (e.g., BSA structuring rules, OFAC screening requirements, FinCEN CTR thresholds). Linking the rule to the obl']
  primary_superseded_by_rule_monitoring_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the monitoring rule that replaces this rule, if applicable.']
  regulatory_exam_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_exam. Business justification: monitoring_rule has regulatory_examination_flag (BOOLEAN) and last_examination_date (DATE) indicating the rule has been reviewed during a regulatory examination. Adding a FK to regulatory_exam provide']
  threshold_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: AML monitoring rules define thresholds in specific currencies; currency reference enables multi-currency normalization, alert generation, and threshold tuning. Core AML transaction monitoring operatio']
  aggregation_method varchar [note: 'Statistical method used to aggregate transaction data for threshold comparison (sum, count, average, maximum, peer comparison).']
  alert_volume_monthly_average varchar [note: 'Average number of alerts generated by this rule per month, used for capacity planning and effectiveness assessment.']
  applicable_customer_segments varchar [note: 'Customer segments or types to which this rule applies (e.g., retail, commercial, private banking, correspondent banking).']
  applicable_product_types varchar [note: 'Banking product types monitored by this rule (e.g., checking accounts, wire transfers, trade finance, securities trading).']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of automation in alert disposition for this rule (fully automated, semi-automated, manual review required).']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Line of business or organizational unit that this rule supports (e.g., Retail Banking, Investment Banking, Wealth Management).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring rule record was first created in the system.']
  detection_logic varchar [note: 'Technical description of the rule logic, including conditions, thresholds, and aggregation methods used for alert generation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this monitoring rule became active and began generating alerts.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this monitoring rule is scheduled to be retired or replaced. Null if no expiration is planned.']
  false_positive_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Calculated percentage of alerts generated by this rule that are determined to be false positives upon investigation.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or jurisdictions where this rule is applied, using ISO 3166 country codes or regional identifiers.']
  last_examination_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory examination that reviewed this rule.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring rule record was last updated.']
  last_tuning_date date [note: 'Date when the rule thresholds or parameters were last adjusted during tuning activities.']
  last_validation_date date [note: 'Date when the rule model was last validated by the Model Risk Management team.']
  lookback_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the historical window analyzed by the rule for pattern detection and aggregation.']
  model_validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation status of the rule model under the Model Risk Management framework.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the detection model or rule logic, following semantic versioning convention.']
  next_validation_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required model validation review.']
  peer_group_definition varchar [note: 'Criteria used to define peer groups for peer-based anomaly detection rules (e.g., industry, geography, account type).']
  regulatory_examination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule has been subject to regulatory examination or scrutiny.']
  retirement_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the rule was retired or deactivated, if applicable.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk severity level associated with alerts generated by this rule.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance category that this rule supports (AML, BSA, OFAC, sanctions, fraud, KYC, CDD). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aml|bsa|ofac|sanctions|fraud|kyc|cdd — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the monitoring rule, used for external reference and system integration.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the monitoring rule logic, detection objective, and business rationale.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the monitoring rule describing its detection purpose.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the monitoring rule in the transaction monitoring system.']
  sar_conversion_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of alerts from this rule that result in SAR filings, indicating rule effectiveness.']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the detection scenario that this rule implements (e.g., structuring, rapid movement, high-risk geography).']
  sla_review_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours within which alerts generated by this rule must be reviewed, per internal SLA policy.']
  threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary threshold value that triggers an alert when exceeded. Used in amount-based detection scenarios.']
  threshold_count varchar [note: 'Transaction count threshold that triggers an alert when exceeded. Used in frequency-based detection scenarios.']
  tuning_history_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this rule has been tuned or recalibrated since initial deployment.']
  tuning_rationale varchar [note: 'Business justification and explanation for the most recent rule tuning or threshold adjustment.']

  Note: 'AML/BSA transaction monitoring rule and threshold configuration record. Captures rule logic, detection scenario, threshold parameters, tuning history, and model validation status.'
}

Table compliance.sanctions_list_entry {
  sanctions_list_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sanctions_list_entry']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Sanctions list entries are country-attributed; compliance teams cross-reference entry country against FATF high-risk flags and OFAC sanctions programs. The existing `country_code` is a denormalized pl']
  issuing_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Sanctions list entries are issued under specific jurisdictional sanctions programs (OFAC/US Treasury, EU, UN, OFSI/UK). The issuing_jurisdiction_id links each entry to its governing jurisdiction, enab']
  parent_sanctions_list_entry_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sanctions_list_entry (parent_sanctions_list_entry_id)']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary address line for the sanctioned party\'s known location or registered address.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details.']
  aircraft_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Manufacturer name of sanctioned aircraft. Null for non-aircraft entries.']
  aircraft_model varchar [note: 'Model designation of sanctioned aircraft. Null for non-aircraft entries.']
  aircraft_tail_number varchar [note: 'Registration or tail number of sanctioned aircraft. Null for non-aircraft entries.']
  alias_names varchar [note: 'Known aliases, alternate spellings, or also-known-as (AKA) names associated with the sanctioned party. Multiple aliases may be concatenated or stored as delimited text.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the sanctioned party\'s known address.']
  data_source_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when the sanctions list data was last published or updated by the source authority.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth for sanctioned individuals. Null for entities, vessels, and aircraft.']
  delisting_date date [note: 'Date on which the entry was removed from the sanctions list, if applicable. Null if still active.']
  designation_date date [note: 'Date on which the individual, entity, vessel, or aircraft was officially designated and added to the sanctions list.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the sanctions entry becomes legally effective and enforceable for compliance screening purposes.']
  entity_registration_number varchar [note: 'Business registration number, company number, or legal entity identifier for sanctioned entities. Null for individuals.']
  entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sanctions list entry indicating whether it is actively enforced, removed, or under review.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the sanctions entry is scheduled to expire or be reviewed, if applicable. Null for indefinite sanctions.']
  is_pep_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the sanctioned individual is also identified as a Politically Exposed Person (PEP) by the sanctions authority.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or update to the sanctions list entry details.']
  list_source_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sanctions list source authority or program (e.g., OFAC SDN, UN Consolidated List, EU Sanctions List, HMT UK Sanctions List).']
  list_type varchar [note: 'Type of sanctioned party: individual person, legal entity, vessel, or aircraft.']
  national_identification_number varchar [note: 'National ID number, tax ID, or other government-issued identification number for sanctioned individuals or entities.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Country of nationality or citizenship for individuals, or country of incorporation/registration for entities. May contain multiple values.']
  passport_number varchar [note: 'Passport number(s) associated with sanctioned individuals. May contain multiple values if multiple passports are known.']
  place_of_birth varchar [note: 'City and country of birth for sanctioned individuals. Null for entities, vessels, and aircraft.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the sanctioned party\'s known address.']
  primary_name varchar [note: 'Primary legal name or designation of the sanctioned individual, entity, vessel, or aircraft as published by the sanctions authority.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific sanctions program under which this entry is listed (e.g., SDGT for Specially Designated Global Terrorist, IRAN for Iran Sanctions).']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Full description of the sanctions program or legal authority under which the entry is designated.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sanctions list entry record was first ingested into the bank\'s compliance system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sanctions list entry record was last updated in the bank\'s compliance system.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, notes, or contextual information provided by the sanctions authority regarding the entry.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned to the sanctions list entry based on severity, program type, and other risk factors. Scale typically 0-100.']
  screening_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned for real-time transaction screening and customer due diligence checks based on risk and regulatory requirements.']
  source_url varchar [note: 'URL or web link to the official sanctions list publication or entry detail page from the issuing authority.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the sanctioned party\'s known address.']
  unique_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official unique identifier assigned by the sanctions authority to this entry (e.g., OFAC SDN number, UN reference number).']
  vessel_call_sign varchar [note: 'Radio call sign assigned to sanctioned vessels. Null for non-vessel entries.']
  vessel_flag varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the flag state under which a sanctioned vessel is registered. Null for non-vessel entries.']
  vessel_imo_number varchar [note: 'IMO ship identification number for sanctioned vessels. Null for non-vessel entries.']
  vessel_tonnage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross tonnage of sanctioned vessels in metric tons. Null for non-vessel entries.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sanctions_list_entry. Referenced by matched_list_entry_id.'
}

Business justification: Channels enforce transaction processing cutoffs and SLA calculations based on business day calendars. Holiday calendars determine when channels process payments and settle transactions. Banking domain']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Channels are licensed and regulated under specific jurisdictions (e.g., FCA, OCC, ECB). Critical for regulatory reporting, compliance validation, and determining applicable capital adequacy and stress']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Banking channels are licensed and operated by specific legal entities.   profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.profit_center. Banking management reporting requires channel-to-profit-cent']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: username_password|biometric|mfa|certificate|oauth|saml|in_person|pin|token — promote to reference product]']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the channel, used for external references and system integration. Follows bank-specific channel coding standards.']

  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the channel record was last updated.']
  omnichannel_orchestration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel participates in omnichannel customer journey orchestration and cross-channel session continuity.']

  product_offering_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of banking products and services available through this channel (all products, restricted subset, or custom configuration).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal region or market code for channel assignment to organizational regions (e.g., Northeast, EMEA, APAC).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions through this channel require specific regulatory reporting (e.g., CTR, SAR, CCAR stress testing).']
  sla_response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Target response time in seconds for channel interactions as defined in the channel SLA (e.g., page load time for digital, call answer time for contact center).']
  sla_uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target uptime percentage for the channel as defined in the SLA (e.g., 99.9% for digital channels).']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Detailed sub-classification of the channel providing granular segmentation (e.g., mobile app vs web banking under digital; full-service branch vs express branch). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mobile_app|web_banking|branch_full_service|branch_express|atm_full_service|atm_deposit_only|ivr|live_agent|chatbot|email|rm_dedicated|rm_shared|open_banking_api|baas_api|embedded_finance — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  supports_account_opening boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel supports new account origination and onboarding workflows.']
  supports_investment_services boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel provides investment banking, wealth management, or securities trading capabilities.']
  supports_loan_origination boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel supports loan application and origination processes.']
  supports_transactions boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel supports financial transaction execution (payments, transfers, withdrawals) or is information/inquiry-only.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the channel was or will be decommissioned and no longer available. Null for active channels.']
  third_party_provider varchar [note: 'Name of third-party vendor or technology provider if the channel is operated or enabled by an external partner (e.g., fintech platform, BaaS provider, core banking vendor). Null if operated in-house.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the channel\'s primary operating location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London).']
  transaction_limit_daily decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate transaction amount allowed per customer per day through this channel. Null if no limit applies.']
  transaction_limit_per_transaction decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum single transaction amount allowed through this channel. Null if no limit applies.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all customer interaction channels operated by the bank, serving as the SSOT for channel identity, classification, and configuration. Covers digital channels (mobile app, web banking), branch network, ATM network, contact center, relationship manager, and API/open banking channels, with support for emerging channel types including embedded finance and Banking-as-a-Service (BaaS). Captures channel type (aligned with ISO 20022 channel type codes where applicable), channel status, capabilities, supported products and services, operating hours, geographic coverage, regulatory classification, and channel-specific configuration parameters. Each channel sub-type (branch, ATM, digital_channel, contact_center) maintains its own detailed master record that FKs back to this registry for unified channel governance, omnichannel orchestration, and enterprise channel performance reporting.'
}

Table channel.branch {
  branch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the branch location. Primary key for the branch entity.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: The channel table is the master registry of ALL customer interaction channels operated by the bank. A branch is a physical instantiation of a channel and must be registered in the channel master. Addi']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Branch physical location country drives AML jurisdiction assignment, FDIC/regulatory exam scheduling, FATCA/CRS reporting obligations, and OFAC sanctions screening. Banking domain experts universally ']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Branches operate with a primary currency for vault management, teller cash balancing, and regulatory reporting (e.g., CTR filings). Banking domain experts expect branches to reference a canonical curr']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.holiday_calendar. Business justification: Branches follow local holiday calendars for operating hours and business day calculations. Critical for SLA compliance, customer service planning, transaction settlement timing, and workforce scheduli']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Branches operate under specific regulatory jurisdictions governing capital adequacy, AML/BSA compliance, and licensing. The regulatory_jurisdiction plain-text column is a denormalized representation. ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Branches operate under a specific legal entity\'s banking license. Regulatory examinations (OCC, FDIC, FRB), branch-level stress testing, and legal entity resolution planning require direct branch-to-l']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the branch location including street number and name.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite number, floor, building name, or other location details.']
  atm_count varchar [note: 'Number of ATM machines located at or immediately adjacent to the branch. Used for self-service capacity planning and customer convenience analysis.']
  branch_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the branch within the bank\'s network. Used for routing, reporting, and regulatory identification.']
  branch_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the branch location as registered with regulatory authorities and displayed to customers.']
  branch_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the branch in its lifecycle. Active branches are fully operational; inactive branches are permanently closed; temporarily closed branches are expected to reopen; pending opening branches are approved but not yet operational; pending closure branches are scheduled for closure; under renovation branches are temporarily closed for facility upgrades.']
  branch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the branch based on service capabilities and operational model. Full-service branches offer complete banking services; limited-service branches offer restricted services; drive-through branches focus on vehicle-accessible transactions; in-store branches are located within retail establishments; mobile branches are temporary or movable units; automated branches are primarily self-service; private banking branches serve high-net-worth clients. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: full_service|limited_service|drive_through|in_store|mobile|automated|private_banking — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  bsa_aml_designation varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the branch for BSA/AML compliance purposes. Standard designation applies default monitoring; high-risk designation requires enhanced due diligence; enhanced monitoring applies additional transaction surveillance; exempt designation applies to branches with limited transaction capabilities.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the branch is located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the branch permanently ceased operations. Null for active branches. Used for network rationalization analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the branch record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  drive_through_lanes varchar [note: 'Number of drive-through service lanes available at the branch for vehicle-accessible banking transactions.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Official email address for the branch used for customer inquiries, service requests, and business correspondence.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Facsimile number for the branch used for document transmission and formal communications.']
  fdic_certificate_number varchar [note: 'FDIC certificate number associated with the institution operating this branch. Used for regulatory reporting and deposit insurance identification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the branch record was most recently updated in the system. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the branch location in decimal degrees format. Used for mapping, proximity analysis, and location-based services.']
  lease_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current lease agreement for the branch facility expires. Null for owned properties. Used for lease renewal planning and real estate strategy.']
  lease_or_owned varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the branch facility is owned by the bank, leased from a third party, or operates under a ground lease arrangement. Used for real estate portfolio management and cost allocation.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the branch location in decimal degrees format. Used for mapping, proximity analysis, and location-based services.']
  market_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the market or metropolitan statistical area (MSA) in which the branch operates. Used for market share analysis and competitive positioning.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date when the branch first opened for business and began serving customers. Used for branch age analysis and network expansion tracking.']
  operating_hours_saturday varchar [note: 'Operating hours for the branch on Saturdays. May differ from weekday hours or be closed. Format includes opening and closing times.']
  operating_hours_sunday varchar [note: 'Operating hours for the branch on Sundays. Many branches are closed on Sundays. Format includes opening and closing times or indication of closure.']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the branch on weekdays (Monday through Friday). Format typically includes opening and closing times (e.g., 09:00-17:00).']
  parking_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dedicated customer parking is available at or near the branch location.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Internal classification of branch performance based on deposit volume, loan origination, customer acquisition, and profitability metrics. Flagship branches are premier locations; Tier 1 represents highest performance; Tier 4 represents lowest performance. Used for resource allocation and strategic planning.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the branch. Used by customers and internal staff for inquiries and service requests.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the branch address used for mail delivery and geographic segmentation.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the geographic region or district to which the branch is assigned for management and reporting purposes.']
  safe_deposit_box_count varchar [note: 'Total number of safe deposit boxes available for customer rental at the branch. Used for vault capacity management and revenue forecasting.']
  square_footage varchar [note: 'Total interior floor space of the branch facility measured in square feet. Used for facilities management, lease cost analysis, and space utilization planning.']
  staff_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of full-time equivalent staff members the branch is designed to accommodate. Used for workforce planning and capacity management.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or primary administrative subdivision where the branch is located.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the branch location (e.g., America/New_York). Used for scheduling, transaction timestamping, and cross-timezone coordination.']
  wheelchair_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the branch facility is fully accessible to customers using wheelchairs, including ramps, elevators, and accessible service counters.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical branch location in the bank\'s network. Captures branch code, name, address, geographic coordinates, branch type (full-service, limited-service, drive-through, in-store), operating hours, staffing capacity, ATM count, safe deposit box availability, accessibility features, regulatory jurisdiction, BSA/AML designation, and branch performance tier. Serves as the SSOT for branch identity and physical network management.'
}

Table channel.atm {
  atm_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ATM unit in the bank\'s owned and managed network.']
  branch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the branch to which this ATM is assigned for operational and reporting purposes. Null if the ATM is not associated with a specific branch.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: The channel table is the master registry of ALL customer interaction channels. An ATM is a self-service channel and must be registered in the channel master alongside digital, branch, and contact-cent']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: ATM operating costs (cash replenishment, maintenance, depreciation, network fees) must be allocated to a cost center for management accounting. Banks track ATM profitability and cost efficiency by cos']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: ATM physical location country determines network affiliation rules, AML monitoring thresholds, surcharge regulations, and regulatory reporting requirements. Banking domain experts expect ATM-to-countr']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: ATMs dispense cash and charge surcharges in a specific currency; cash replenishment planning, interchange settlement, and regulatory reporting require a validated currency reference. Banking domain ex']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.holiday_calendar. Business justification: ATMs follow holiday calendars for maintenance scheduling, cash replenishment planning, and service availability. Critical for operational efficiency, cash management, and ensuring adequate liquidity d']
  ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ATM meets ADA accessibility requirements (True) or not (False).']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line where the ATM is physically located.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit number where the ATM is located.']
  average_daily_cash_dispensed decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average amount of cash dispensed by this ATM per day in the local currency, calculated over a rolling period.']
  average_daily_transactions varchar [note: 'Average number of transactions processed by this ATM per day, calculated over a rolling period (e.g., 30 days).']
  biometric_authentication_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ATM supports biometric authentication methods such as fingerprint or iris scanning (True) or not (False).']
  cash_cassette_configuration varchar [note: 'Denomination configuration of cash cassettes (e.g., Cassette 1: $20, Cassette 2: $50, Cassette 3: $100, Cassette 4: $20).']
  cash_cassette_count varchar [note: 'Number of cash cassettes installed in the ATM for dispensing currency.']
  cash_replenishment_schedule varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the ATM is scheduled for cash replenishment: daily, weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, or on-demand based on usage.']
  check_imaging_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ATM has check imaging capability for remote deposit capture (True) or not (False).']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the ATM is located.']
  contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ATM supports contactless card or mobile wallet transactions (True) or not (False).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ATM record was first created in the system.']
  daily_withdrawal_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative withdrawal amount per cardholder per day at this ATM in the local currency.']
  deposit_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ATM supports cash and/or check deposit functionality (True) or is withdrawal-only (False).']
  encryption_standard varchar [note: 'Encryption standard used by the ATM for securing transaction data (e.g., 3DES, AES-128, AES-256).']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the firmware currently installed on the ATM.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the ATM was first installed and commissioned at its current location.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Network IP address assigned to the ATM for communication with the bank\'s transaction processing systems.']
  last_cash_replenishment_date date [note: 'Date when the ATM was last replenished with cash.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent scheduled or unscheduled maintenance service performed on the ATM.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the ATM location in decimal degrees.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ATM placement: branch-attached (within or adjacent to a branch), off-premise (remote location), in-lobby, drive-through, or standalone kiosk.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the ATM location in decimal degrees.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the ATM unit.']
  max_withdrawal_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum single transaction withdrawal amount allowed at this ATM in the local currency.']
  model varchar [note: 'Model designation of the ATM unit as specified by the manufacturer (e.g., NCR SelfServ 80, Diebold Nixdorf DN Series).']
  network_affiliations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of interbank networks the ATM participates in (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, STAR, Allpoint, NYCE, Cirrus).']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the next scheduled preventive maintenance is planned for the ATM.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the ATM: active (in service), inactive (temporarily offline), maintenance (scheduled service), out-of-service (unplanned downtime), or decommissioned (permanently removed).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the ATM location.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number uniquely identifying the physical ATM hardware unit.']
  service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party service provider responsible for ATM maintenance and cash management, if outsourced. Null if managed in-house.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the ATM is located.']
  supported_transaction_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of transaction types the ATM supports (e.g., withdrawal, deposit, balance inquiry, transfer, bill payment, check cashing).']
  surcharge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to non-customer cardholders for using this ATM, in the local currency. Zero if no surcharge applies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ATM record was last modified in the system.']
  video_banking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ATM supports live video teller assistance (True) or not (False).']

  Note: 'Master record for each ATM unit in the bank\'s owned and managed network. Captures ATM ID, serial number, model, manufacturer, location (branch-attached or off-premise), address, geographic coordinates, network affiliations (Visa/Mastercard/STAR/Allpoint), supported transaction types, cash cassette configuration, surcharge policy, accessibility compliance (ADA), operational status, last maintenance date, and cash replenishment schedule.'
}

Table channel.digital_channel {
  digital_channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the digital channel instance, registered API consumer, or third-party provider application.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Digital channel instances should reference their parent channel definition in the master channel registry. This resolves the silo issue for digital_channel. The FK links each digital banking platform ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Digital channel infrastructure costs (cloud hosting, API gateway, cybersecurity, development) are allocated to cost centers for technology cost management and IT chargeback processes. Banks track digi']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Digital channels operate under specific regulatory jurisdictions (PSD2, eIDAS, CFPB 1033). Critical for TPP registration validation, API governance, consent management, and regulatory compliance in op']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Digital channels (mobile banking, internet banking, open banking APIs) are licensed per legal entity under PSD2, OCC, and national banking regulations. Regulatory reporting and conduct supervision req']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.profit_center. Business justification: Digital channels generate fee income and drive product sales attributed to profit centers for segment P&L reporting. IFRS 8 segment disclosure and management reporting require digital channel revenue ']
  transaction_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Digital channels enforce transaction limits in specific currencies. Critical for PSD2 compliance, API rate limiting, cross-border payment processing, and multi-currency digital banking operations.']
  api_gateway_endpoint varchar [note: 'Base URL or endpoint address for the API gateway serving this channel.']
  api_product_subscriptions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of subscribed API products (e.g., \'account_information\', \'payment_initiation\', \'funds_confirmation\', \'card_management\').']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of supported authentication methods (e.g., biometric, MFA, OTP, OAuth2, OIDC, certificate).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this digital channel record was first created in the system.']
  data_access_scope varchar [note: 'OAuth2 scope or permission set defining the data and operations this channel or TPP is authorized to access.']
  developer_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for technical contact or developer responsible for this channel or API consumer.']
  developer_organization_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the organization or developer entity that owns or operates this API consumer or channel.']
  digital_channel_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the digital channel instance or API consumer registration.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this digital channel configuration or API consumer registration becomes active and operational.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this digital channel configuration or API consumer registration remains valid; null for open-ended registrations.']
  eidas_certificate_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the eIDAS (electronic IDentification, Authentication and trust Services) qualified certificate used for TPP authentication.']
  encryption_standard varchar [note: 'Encryption protocol or standard required for secure communication with this channel.']
  environment varchar [note: 'Deployment environment classification for the channel instance.']
  feature_flags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of enabled feature flags or capabilities for this channel instance (e.g., \'payment_initiation\', \'account_aggregation\', \'funds_confirmation\').']
  ip_whitelist varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of whitelisted IP addresses or CIDR ranges authorized to access this channel or API endpoint.']
  is_baas_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel is configured for Banking-as-a-Service or embedded finance capabilities.']
  last_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent API call, login, or transaction activity through this channel.']
  max_requests_per_minute varchar [note: 'Maximum number of API requests allowed per minute for this channel instance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this digital channel record was last modified or updated.']
  oauth_client_identifier varchar [note: 'OAuth2 or OIDC client identifier for API consumer authentication.']
  oauth_client_secret_hash varchar [note: 'Hashed OAuth2 client secret for secure credential storage.']
  onboarding_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the channel or API consumer onboarding workflow.']
  onboarding_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the approver who authorized this channel or API consumer registration.']
  onboarding_approved_date date [note: 'Date when the channel or API consumer registration was approved.']
  platform_variant varchar [note: 'Specific platform or protocol variant for the channel (e.g., iOS, Android, REST API).']
  rate_limit_tier varchar [note: 'API rate limiting tier assigned to this channel or consumer, governing request throughput.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to this channel or TPP based on security assessment, transaction patterns, and regulatory compliance.']
  session_timeout_minutes varchar [note: 'Idle session timeout duration in minutes before automatic logout or token expiration.']
  sla_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Target API response time in milliseconds as defined in the SLA for this channel.']
  sla_uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target uptime percentage (e.g., 99.95) as defined in the SLA for this channel.']
  terms_accepted_date date [note: 'Date when the current terms of service version was accepted by the developer or TPP.']
  terms_of_service_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terms of service or API usage agreement accepted by the channel or TPP.']
  tpp_registration_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory registration or license number for third-party provider applications under PSD2 or open banking frameworks.']
  transaction_limit_daily decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value of transactions allowed per day through this channel.']
  transaction_limit_per_transaction decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value allowed for a single transaction through this channel.']
  version varchar [note: 'Current version number or release identifier of the digital channel application or API endpoint.']
  webhook_url varchar [note: 'Callback URL endpoint for asynchronous event notifications or webhooks sent to the API consumer.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record for each digital banking channel instance, registered API consumer, and third-party provider (TPP) application — mobile banking app (iOS/Android), web banking portal, open banking API endpoints, aggregator integrations, and embedded finance/BaaS API consumers. Captures platform type, version, authentication methods (biometric, MFA, OTP), supported transaction limits, session timeout policies, feature flags, API gateway endpoint, OAuth2/OIDC client credentials (hashed), rate limit tier, environment (sandbox/UAT/production), PSD2/open banking compliance status, TPP regulatory registration (license number, jurisdiction), eIDAS certificate reference, developer/organization identity, API product subscriptions (account information, payment initiation, funds confirmation), data access scope, terms of service version accepted, onboarding approval status, and channel-specific SLA parameters. SSOT for digital channel configuration, API consumer identity, access governance, and TPP lifecycle management.'
}

Table channel.session {
  session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for session']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the channel through which the session was initiated (mobile app, web banking, branch, ATM, contact center, relationship manager, API).']
  geolocation_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Session geolocation country is used for AML country-risk scoring and fraud detection — referencing country.aml_risk_rating and ofac_sanctions_flag directly. Banking domain experts expect geolocation c']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or party associated with this session. Null for anonymous sessions.']
  application_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the mobile or desktop application used for the session. Null for web browser sessions.']
  authentication_level varchar [note: 'Strength level of authentication applied, aligned with regulatory requirements for customer due diligence and transaction authorization.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate the customer at session initiation (password, biometric, one-time password, security token, digital certificate, single sign-on).']
  browser_name varchar [note: 'Name of the web browser used for the session (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge). Null for non-browser channels.']
  browser_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the web browser used for the session. Null for non-browser channels.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this session record was first created in the system.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or hash representing the device characteristics used to access the session. Used for fraud detection and device recognition.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the device used to initiate the session (mobile phone, tablet, desktop computer, ATM, kiosk, point-of-sale terminal).']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the session in seconds, calculated as the difference between session end and start timestamps.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session was terminated, either by user action, timeout, or system event.']
  exit_page_url varchar [note: 'URL of the last page or screen accessed before the session was terminated.']
  fraud_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Real-time fraud risk score assigned to the session based on behavioral analytics, device fingerprinting, and transaction patterns. Scale 0-100, higher indicates greater risk.']
  geolocation_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the session origin, derived from IP address or device GPS. Used for location-based fraud detection and customer journey analysis.']
  geolocation_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the session origin, derived from IP address or device GPS. Used for location-based fraud detection and customer journey analysis.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the session was initiated. Used for geolocation, fraud detection, and security monitoring.']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'URL of the first page or screen accessed in the session.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO language code representing the customer\'s preferred language for the session interface.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this session record was last updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments captured during the session, typically used for assisted channels such as contact center or relationship manager interactions.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device used during the session (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows, macOS).']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome classification of the session indicating whether the customer completed their intended action, abandoned the session, experienced a timeout, or encountered an error.']
  page_view_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pages or screens viewed during the session. Used for engagement analysis and customer journey mapping.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page or source that referred the customer to initiate the session. Used for marketing attribution and customer journey analysis.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the session.']
  session_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the session based on authentication and interaction mode.']
  sla_actual_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Actual average response time in milliseconds measured during the session.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the session met the defined SLA targets for response time and availability.']
  sla_target_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Target response time in milliseconds defined by the SLA for this session type and channel.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session was initiated by the customer or system.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed reason for session termination, including user logout, inactivity timeout, security event, system error, or forced logout.']
  token varchar [note: 'Unique cryptographic token assigned to the session for authentication and authorization purposes. Used to maintain session state and security.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of financial or non-financial transactions executed within the session.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user agent string identifying the client software, operating system, and device type used to access the session.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each authenticated or anonymous customer session across all channels — mobile app, web banking, branch visit, ATM interaction, and contact center call. Captures session ID, channel reference, customer identifier, device fingerprint, IP address, geolocation, authentication method and level, session start/end timestamps, session duration, pages/screens visited, transaction count within session, session outcome (completed, abandoned, timed-out), and termination reason. Serves as the primary grain for fraud detection behavioral models, omnichannel journey tracking, and session-level SLA measurement. Links to channel_interaction for detailed event-level analysis.'
}

Table channel.interaction {
  interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for interaction']
  atm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.atm. Business justification: ATM interactions require direct ATM FK for transaction reconciliation, ATM performance analytics, cash management, and incident correlation. Removes denormalized atm_network_code in favor of proper FK']
  branch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.branch. Business justification: The interaction table currently stores branch_location_code as a denormalized STRING, which is a normalization violation. Replacing it with a proper FK branch_id -> branch.branch_id allows joining to ']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: interaction currently has channel_type (STRING) but no FK to the channel master table. Each interaction event occurs through a specific channel instance and must be linked to channel for proper channe']
  corporate_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.corporate_action. Business justification: Corporate action servicing is a named banking process: customers contact the bank via branch, digital, or phone to inquire about or elect on dividend payments, rights issues, mergers, or tender offers']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Interactions record transaction amounts for CTR threshold monitoring and AML reporting — both require a validated currency reference to apply correct thresholds and convert to reporting currency. Bank']
  deposit_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the account involved in this interaction, if applicable.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or party who initiated or is associated with this interaction.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the customer session within which this interaction occurred, enabling journey reconstruction across multiple interactions.']
  api_endpoint varchar [note: 'The API endpoint URL or path invoked during the interaction. Populated only for API channel interactions.']
  api_payload_size_bytes varchar [note: 'The size of the API request or response payload in bytes. Populated only for API channel interactions.']
  api_response_code varchar [note: 'The HTTP response code returned by the API endpoint (e.g., 200, 400, 500). Populated only for API channel interactions.']
  atm_authorization_code varchar [note: 'The authorization or approval code returned by the ATM network for the transaction. Populated only for ATM channel interactions.']
  atm_cash_dispensed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cash was successfully dispensed during the ATM transaction. Populated only for ATM channel interactions.']
  atm_response_code varchar [note: 'The response code returned by the ATM network indicating success, decline, or error condition. Populated only for ATM channel interactions.']
  atm_surcharge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The surcharge fee applied to the ATM transaction, if applicable. Populated only for ATM channel interactions.']
  contact_center_call_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The total duration of the contact center call in seconds. Populated only for contact center voice interactions.']
  contact_center_interaction_mode varchar [note: 'The mode of contact center interaction (e.g., Interactive Voice Response (IVR), live voice call, chat, email, Short Message Service (SMS)). Populated only for contact center channel interactions.']
  contact_center_ivr_path varchar [note: 'The sequence of IVR menu selections made by the customer during the interaction. Populated only for contact center IVR interactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this interaction record was first created in the system.']
  ctr_threshold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction amount meets or exceeds the $10,000 threshold requiring a Currency Transaction Report (CTR) filing under Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) and Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) regulations. Populated for branch and other cash transactions.']
  digital_device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used for digital banking interactions. Populated only for digital banking channel interactions.']
  digital_session_ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the digital banking session originated. Populated only for digital banking channel interactions.']
  digital_user_agent varchar [note: 'The user agent string identifying the browser or application used for the digital banking interaction. Populated only for digital banking channel interactions.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The duration of the interaction in seconds, from initiation to completion or termination.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'The system error code if the interaction failed or encountered an error condition.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'The human-readable error message describing the failure or error condition.']
  geolocation_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The latitude coordinate of the interaction location, if captured (e.g., for mobile banking or ATM transactions).']
  geolocation_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The longitude coordinate of the interaction location, if captured (e.g., for mobile banking or ATM transactions).']
  interaction_status varchar [note: 'The outcome or current status of the interaction event.']
  interaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the interaction event occurred in the channel system.']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of interaction or transaction performed (e.g., withdrawal, deposit, balance inquiry, login, transfer, bill payment, wire initiation, account inquiry, IVR navigation, agent call, API call).']
  product_service_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the product or service associated with this interaction (e.g., checking account, savings account, loan product, investment product).']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the customer inquiry or issue was resolved during this interaction.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interaction met the defined SLA target time.']
  sla_target_seconds varchar [note: 'The target service level agreement time for this interaction type, measured in seconds.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount involved in the interaction, if applicable (e.g., withdrawal amount, deposit amount, transfer amount).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this interaction record was last updated in the system.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each discrete customer interaction event across any channel, serving as the single source of truth for all channel-originated transaction and engagement activity. Uses a channel_type discriminator to capture channel-specific attributes: ATM transactions (withdrawal, deposit, balance inquiry, cardless cash, PIN change — with network, authorization/response codes, surcharge, cash dispensed flag), branch teller transactions (cash deposit, withdrawal, check cashing, wire initiation, cashiers checks, foreign currency exchange, safe deposit box access — with teller ID, CTR threshold flag for BSA/FinCEN $10K reporting, override approvals), digital banking actions (login, transfer, bill pay, account inquiry), contact center interactions (IVR path, agent interaction, chat, email), and API calls (endpoint, response code, payload size). Captures interaction type, channel reference, timestamp, duration, amount (where applicable), currency, outcome, associated product/service, staff member (for branch/RM), resolution status, and session reference. Enables omnichannel journey reconstruction, SLA measurement, regulatory transaction reporting (CTR/SAR), and channel performance analytics.'
}

Table channel.atm_transaction {
  atm_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ATM transaction event. Primary key for the atm_transaction product.']
  atm_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ATM terminal where the transaction was executed. Links to the ATM device master record.']
  card_id varchar [note: 'Tokenized reference to the card used for the transaction. Links to the card master record without exposing PAN (Primary Account Number).']
  channel_session_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the ATM session during which this transaction occurred, used to group multiple transactions within a single customer session.']
  chargeback_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.chargeback. Business justification: Visa/Mastercard dispute rules require the original ATM transaction reference when filing ATM chargebacks. ATM dispute processing workflows must directly link the disputed ATM transaction to its charge']
  deposit_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account debited or credited by this ATM transaction.']
  interaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to channel.interaction. Business justification: The interaction table is the master transactional record of each discrete customer interaction event across any channel. An ATM transaction IS a customer interaction event — it generates an interactio']
  original_transaction_atm_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original ATM transaction ID if this record represents a reversal or adjustment. Null for original transactions.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (party) who initiated the ATM transaction. Links to the customer master record.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: ATM withdrawals and deposits are payment transactions requiring direct link for reconciliation, settlement, dispute resolution, and financial reporting. Core operational relationship between ATM chann']
  primary_atm_issuer_institution_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Bank Identification Number (BIN) or institution code of the issuing bank that issued the card used in the transaction.']
  transaction_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: ATM transaction currency is required for interchange fee calculation, CTR threshold monitoring, and regulatory reporting. Banking domain experts expect ATM transaction currency to reference the canoni']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code returned by the issuing bank or card network upon successful authorization of the transaction.']
  card_entry_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which the card data was captured at the ATM: chip (EMV chip read), magnetic stripe, contactless (NFC/RFID), or manual entry.']
  cash_dispensed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount of cash dispensed by the ATM, which should match the transaction amount for withdrawal transactions. Null for non-cash transactions.']
  cash_dispensed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether physical cash was successfully dispensed by the ATM for this transaction (True=cash dispensed, False=no cash dispensed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transaction record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the customer has filed a dispute or chargeback claim for this transaction. True=dispute filed, False=no dispute.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this transaction was flagged as potentially fraudulent by the fraud detection system. True=flagged for review, False=not flagged.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned by the fraud detection system indicating the likelihood of fraudulent activity, typically ranging from 0 (low risk) to 100 (high risk).']
  interchange_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee paid by the acquiring bank to the issuing bank for processing the ATM transaction, typically a percentage of the transaction amount plus a fixed fee.']
  network_code varchar [note: 'Card network or ATM network used to route and authorize the transaction (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, STAR, Cirrus, Plus). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visa|mastercard|star|pulse|cirrus|plus|interac|maestro — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  on_us_transaction_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this is an on-us transaction (customer using their own bank\'s ATM) or an off-us transaction (customer using another bank\'s ATM). True=on-us, False=off-us.']
  pin_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the customer\'s PIN was successfully verified during the transaction. True=PIN verified, False=PIN not verified or failed.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction was posted to the customer\'s account in the core banking system, may differ from transaction date due to batch processing.']
  receipt_printed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a paper receipt was printed for the customer (True=receipt printed, False=receipt declined or unavailable).']
  response_code varchar [note: 'Numeric code returned by the issuer or network indicating the outcome of the authorization request (e.g., 00=approved, 51=insufficient funds, 54=expired card).']
  response_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the response code, explaining the reason for approval or decline (e.g., \'Approved\', \'Insufficient Funds\', \'Invalid PIN\').']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this transaction was reversed due to timeout, cash dispenser failure, or other technical issue. True=transaction reversed, False=not reversed.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount settled for this transaction, including transaction amount, surcharge, and any applicable fees, in the settlement currency.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement amount, which may differ from transaction currency for cross-border transactions.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction will be or was settled between the acquiring and issuing banks, typically T+0 or T+1 from transaction date.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional fee charged by the ATM owner for out-of-network transactions, displayed to the customer before transaction approval.']
  terminal_location_code varchar [note: 'Geographic or branch location code where the ATM terminal is physically installed, used for location-based analytics and fraud detection.']
  terminal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ATM terminal type: branch (inside bank branch), offsite (standalone location), mobile (portable ATM), kiosk (self-service banking kiosk), or drive-through.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Principal monetary amount of the ATM transaction in the transaction currency, excluding any surcharges or fees.']
  transaction_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique reference number for the ATM transaction, used for customer inquiries and dispute resolution.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ATM transaction: approved (successful), declined (rejected by issuer or network), reversed (voided after initial approval), pending (awaiting settlement), timeout (no response received), or cancelled (customer aborted).']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ATM transaction was initiated by the customer at the terminal, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of ATM transaction performed: cash withdrawal, deposit, balance inquiry, PIN change, mini statement, cardless cash, inter-bank transfer, or bill payment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: withdrawal|deposit|balance_inquiry|pin_change|mini_statement|cardless_cash|inter_bank_transfer|bill_payment — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transaction record was last modified in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each ATM transaction event — cash withdrawal, deposit, balance inquiry, PIN change, cardless cash, and inter-bank transfers. Captures ATM ID, card number (tokenized), customer reference, transaction type, amount, currency, network used (Visa/Mastercard/STAR), authorization code, response code, surcharge applied, cash dispensed flag, timestamp, and transaction status. Distinct from payment domain wire/ACH — this is ATM-channel-specific card-present transaction capture.'
}

Table reference.currency {
  currency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the currency record. Primary key.']
  benchmark_rate_type varchar [note: 'The primary interest rate benchmark associated with this currency (e.g., Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR) for USD, Euro Short-Term Rate (ESTR) for EUR, Sterling Overnight Index Average (SONIA) for GBP). Used for pricing loans, derivatives, and other financial instruments. Reflects the transition from London Interbank Offered Rate (LIBOR) to alternative reference rates.']
  central_bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the central bank or monetary authority responsible for issuing and regulating this currency (e.g., Federal Reserve System for USD, European Central Bank for EUR, Bank of England for GBP). Used for regulatory reporting and monetary policy reference.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this currency record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the currency (e.g., United States Dollar, Euro, British Pound Sterling, Japanese Yen).']
  currency_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the currency. Active currencies are currently in circulation and available for transactions. Inactive currencies are temporarily suspended. Deprecated currencies are being phased out (e.g., legacy currencies replaced by EUR). Historical currencies are no longer in use but retained for historical transaction reference. Pending currencies are approved but not yet in circulation.']
  currency_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the currency by its fundamental nature. Fiat currencies are government-issued legal tender. Commodity currencies are backed by physical commodities. Digital currencies include cryptocurrencies and central bank digital currencies. Fund represents investment fund units. Special Drawing Right (SDR) is the International Monetary Fund (IMF) reserve asset.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this currency record becomes effective and available for use in financial transactions. For new currencies, this is the introduction date. For currency updates, this is the date the change takes effect.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this currency record is effective. Nullable for currently active currencies with no planned end date. For deprecated or historical currencies, this represents the date the currency ceased to be legal tender or was replaced.']
  is_base_currency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency serves as a base currency for the enterprise\'s financial reporting and consolidation. Typically the functional currency of the parent company. Used for General Ledger (GL) consolidation and financial statement preparation.']
  is_convertible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency is freely convertible on international foreign exchange markets without significant restrictions. True for major reserve currencies and freely traded currencies. False for currencies with limited convertibility or capital controls.']
  is_crypto_currency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a cryptocurrency or digital asset rather than a fiat currency issued by a central bank. Used to apply different regulatory treatment, risk controls, and processing rules for digital assets.']
  is_fund_currency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency code represents a fund or investment vehicle unit rather than a traditional currency. Used for mutual funds, exchange-traded funds, and other investment products that have their own unit pricing.']
  is_legal_tender boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency is currently recognized as legal tender in its issuing jurisdiction. True for currencies that must be accepted for payment of debts. False for commemorative, historical, or non-legal tender currencies.']
  is_precious_metal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency code represents a precious metal (e.g., XAU for gold, XAG for silver, XPT for platinum) used as a store of value or trading instrument. ISO 4217 includes codes for precious metals alongside traditional currencies.']
  is_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this currency is subject to capital controls, exchange restrictions, or regulatory limitations that restrict its convertibility or cross-border movement. True for currencies with government-imposed restrictions. Used for compliance and risk management.']
  iso_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter alphabetic currency code as defined by ISO 4217 standard (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). This is the primary business identifier used across all financial systems for currency denomination.']
  issuing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country or jurisdiction that issues this currency (e.g., USA for USD, DEU for EUR in Germany context). For supranational currencies like EUR, this may represent the primary issuing authority or be left as a representative member state.']
  last_revaluation_date date [note: 'Date when currency balances denominated in this currency were last revalued for financial reporting purposes. Used in foreign currency revaluation processes for General Ledger (GL) and to track compliance with accounting standards requiring periodic revaluation.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this currency record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization across systems.']
  minor_unit varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places used for the currency\'s minor unit. Indicates the relationship between the major currency unit and its minor unit (e.g., 2 for USD cents, 0 for JPY which has no minor unit, 3 for certain Middle Eastern currencies). Critical for accurate financial calculations and rounding.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information, special handling instructions, regulatory notes, or historical context about the currency. May include information about currency reforms, redenominations, or special processing requirements.']
  numeric_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit numeric currency code as defined by ISO 4217 standard (e.g., 840 for USD, 978 for EUR). Used in systems where alphabetic codes are not suitable.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating the relative importance or usage frequency of this currency within the enterprise. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used to determine default currencies, optimize system performance, and prioritize currency pair availability in FX trading systems.']
  rounding_method varchar [note: 'Standard rounding method to be applied for this currency in financial calculations. Standard uses mathematical rounding (0.5 rounds up). Bankers rounding rounds 0.5 to the nearest even number. Up always rounds up. Down always rounds down. Truncate drops decimal places without rounding. Critical for ensuring consistent calculation results across systems.']
  settlement_lag_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of business days required for settlement of foreign exchange (FX) transactions involving this currency. Typically 2 days (T+2) for major currencies, but can vary. Used for calculating settlement dates in FX trading and payment processing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system or data provider from which this currency record originated (e.g., Core Banking System, Market Data Provider, ISO 4217 Registry). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  symbol varchar [note: 'Graphical symbol used to represent the currency in financial displays and reports (e.g., $, €, £, ¥). Used for user interface presentation and customer-facing documents.']
